card.desc:
# **Locomotion**

The main way Survivors maneuver around the map is a collection of things, such as **Stamina Management, Movement States, Interactables** such as **Vaults,** and more.

## **Movement States**

There are multiple movement states, being **Walking, Sprinting, Exhausted, Fatigue,** and **Crouching.**

- _Walking: Default State. Move at **16 Stud/s.**_
- _Sprinting: Using Stamina to move at **160% Walk Speed.**_
- _Exhausted: Innate **15% Hinder.** Temporary State when out of Stamina. Expires upon recovering Stamina._
- _Fatigue: Innate **25% Hinder.** Temporary State when Running for over **4s.**_
- _Crouching: Move at **40% Walk Speed** but become silent._

## **Stamina**

Stamina is not standardized, instead being a stat for each character which can vary. Stamina is defined by **Max Stamina, Stamina Drain,** and **Stamina Regen.**

There is a **0.6s Delay** before Stamina begins to regenerate.

Characters, upon running out of Stamina, have a **2.5s Delay** before Stamina begins to regenerate once more. If you enter the **Fatigue State,** this delay is increased to **3.5s.**

**Stamina Usage** is **Locked** if Stamina falls below **20%** of **Max Stamina,** and cannot be used again until exceeding that threshold by regenerating Stamina. This does not apply if you continue sprinting when passing the threshold.

## **Interactables**

Interactables are specific **Map Features** that can be interacted with to perform some kind of movement action. This includes things like **Vaults,** taking the form of **Windows, Pallets,** or other miscellaneous objects.
card.name: Locomotion, Stamina, Interactables

old.desc:
# **Health States & Pools of Blood**

While at **100% Health**, you are considered in the “**Healthy” State.**

- Below 50% Health: Bleeding effect and leave behind **Pools of Blood** every **1 Second** wherever you go. Pools of Blood persist for **3.5 Seconds.**
- Below 40% Health: Enters the "Injured" State with heavy panting. You will hold your gut while in this state.

## **Healing Actions**

**Healing Actions** are any actions taken by **Survivors** to heal themselves or others. These actions include; **Healing (Self or Altruistically), Mending (Fatal Wounds),** or **Recovering (Altruistically).** **Self-Healing** actions progress at **33% Speed.**

- _Healing takes **12s.** (20s Self)_
- _Mending takes a **Fixed 6s.** (10s Self)_
- _Recovering takes **16s.** (27s Self, 95% Cap)_

**Self-Healing** can only be performed with a **Med-Kit** or having a **Perk or Passive** that allows self-healing actions.

**Healing Actions** will prompt **Skill-Checks** occasionally with a **8% Chance** of appearing **every second.**
Missing a **Healing Skill-Check** will trigger a **Loud Noise Notification** at your location.

## **Ticks, Downed State, & Recovery**

At **0 Health,** enter the ‘_Downed’_ State and lose a _'tick.'_

Survivors can be downed a total of **Two Times** before **Dying** if downed a third time. This is shown on the **UI** as ‘_ticks,’_ with Survivors losing a tick each time they are downed, displayed as the tick **burning away.** Upon losing both ticks, a bloody strike will be seen struck through the ticks, symbolizing that it’s your last chance.

While in the **Downed State,** a **240s Timer** will begin ticking down, killing you instantly upon expiry, regardless of your ticks. This timer will continue where left off if Downed again.
Survivors are able to **Fully Recover** to pick themselves up **once** per round.

## **Death**

**Death** is the final state of a Survivor. Dying will remove you from the match. Dying can happen in a multitude of ways, such as **Bleeding Out** when downed, being **Requiemed,** getting downed with **No Ticks,** or through various **Perk Interactions.**

Upon dying, you will immediately **drop all of your items.**

old.desc:
# **Survivor Archetypes**

## **Altruistic**

Survivors who excel in helping their teammates, be it by healing, buffs, or generally selfless actions that aid the team directly.

## **Confrontational**

Survivors who excel in head-on interactions with the Killer, be it fighting them, defensive actions, or generally offensive actions against the Killer.

## **Operator**

Survivors who excel in completing Objectives, often having abilities that interact with Objectives or grant themselves and/or others objective proficiency.

## **Preservation**

Survivors who excel in self-preservation, often having abilities that allow them to escape chase or prolong their own survival in one way or another.

## **Stealth**

Survivors who excel in avoiding the Killer and entities alike, having abilities that grant them stealth in one way or another.

## **Technical**

Survivors with more esoteric abilities that don’t fit in the other categories, often having special powers with special interactions.

card.desc:
# **Survivor Archetypes**

## **Support**

Survivors who excel in helping their teammates, be it by healing, buffs, or providing support in some kind of other way such as traps or buildings.

## **Confrontational**

Survivors who excel in head-on interactions with the Killer, be it fighting them, defensive actions, or generally offensive actions against the Killer.

## **Operator**

Survivors who excel in completing Objectives, often having abilities that interact with Objectives or grant themselves and/or others objective proficiency.

## **Preservation**

Survivors who excel in self-preservation, often having abilities that allow them to escape chase or prolong their own survival in one way or another.

## **Stealth**

Survivors who excel in avoiding the Killer and entities alike, having abilities that grant them stealth in one way or another.

## **Technical**

Survivors with more esoteric abilities that don’t fit in the other categories, often having special powers with special interactions.
card.name: Survivor Archetypes

card.desc:
# **Main Goal [Survivor]**

As a Survivor, your main goal is to **stay alive** and **neutralize the Killer**, with your secondary goals being to **Complete all Objectives** and escape with your team. You start with **Two of each Material** in your Backpack/Inventory, accessible by clicking Q while in-game.
✱ _Some maps may have alternative methods for opening the Exit._
✱ _Keybinds can be customized in the settings._

To achieve a Survivor Win, the **Killer must be neutralized** or all objectives must be completed with more than half of the Survivors escaping. Otherwise, it will end in a draw.

Dealing with the Killer can be accomplished through a variety of ways, with **Weapons** and **Tools** spawning on the map alongside **Materials** which can be used to **Craft** more weapons and tools, which include things from **Pistols** to **Rifles** and **Traps.**

As for Objectives, they will **vary between maps**, alongside special **Objective Phases.** The specifics for each Objective differ, and some are only available during certain game stages. Objective Phases advance by completing necessary Objectives displayed in the corner of your screen. Additionally, objectives will scale with player count, with the length of objectives and amount changing accordingly.
card.name: Main Goal

old.desc:
# **Game Concept**

E://R is a 1v9 Asymmetrical Juggernaut-style Slasher Horror game with a large amount of gameplay, varying from combat/gunplay, objectives, stealth, and looting. It has you playing one of the titular roles, being Survivor or Killer.

The end goal for Survivors is neutralizing the killer, looting the map for supplies, weapons, and tools while helping your fellow Survivors and completing objectives to give more time all while trying to survive the killer’s onslaught.

The end goal for Killers is the death of all Survivors, preventing them from completing objectives while slaughtering them all.

The game has many different inspirations, namely that of Dead by Daylight, Trepidation, Still Wakes The Deep, the Resident Evil Franchise, BASILISK / BASILISK - 2000, Dead Space, Project Moon, Risk of Rain, Left 4 Dead, Escape The Darkness, web series such as Vita Carnis, GREYLOCK, Mandela Catalogue, Gemini Home Entertainment, and other various inspirations such as SCP. The core concept of the gameplay is inspired by Resident Evil, Dead by Daylight, and Escape The Darkness.
My main goal with this is to create a fun game that I can enjoy with my friends, while introducing many concepts and ideas for features in this genre of game that people have not seen before! I hope this turns out to be an enjoyable game for everyone, further inspire games in the future, and hopefully introduce new standards & expectations for the Asymmetrical game genre.

# **Basic Info**

Both Survivors & Killers can toggle between **First & Third Person.**

There is **player collision**, meaning that **Survivors** and **Killers** can collide with one another, alongside **Survivor to Survivor** collision. This may not be permanent, or may be disabled at some point if **Exploiters** become an issue.

And for Christ's sake, _all canon characters are 18+._

old.name: Main Goal (Attack Cooldowns, Preparation Phase, Downs, Kills, Chase, etc.)

old.desc:
# **Main Goal [Killer]**

As a Killer, you main goal is to Kill all Survivors. You have many tools at your disposal to do so, such as your **Killer Powers**.

To achieve a Killer Win, **all Survivors must die** or the timer runs out with more than half of the Survivors dead.

## **Standard Attacks**

**Standard Attacks** are your main tool as a **Killer**, allowing you to use your LMB/M1/Click perform a basic attack on **Survivors** to deal damage.

You may also hold down the LMB/M1 button to perform a **Lunge,** which will will cause the Killer to lunge forward, boosting the Killer’s current movement speed by **1.5x** for **1.2 Seconds** and increasing the attack window. The lunge can be stopped at any time, swinging upon ending it. The Killer retains full maneuverability during a lunge, allowing them to swing around corners and obstacles.
The Cooldowns of Successful Standard Attacks are listed in each Killer’s Card, but all missed Basic Attacks will be **1 Second.** The cooldown for a missed lunge is **1.5 Seconds.**

However, the Slow-down for Standard Attacks are listed as such below;

- _Successful Basic Attack; -36% Movement Speed/64% Movement Speed._
- _Missed Basic Attack; -24% Movement Speed/76% Movement Speed._
- _Successful Lunge Attack; -48% Movement Speed/52% Movement Speed._
- _Missed Lunge Attack; -36% Movement Speed/64% Movement Speed._

Entities do not have any slowdown on attacks unless specified.

## **Preparation Phase**

The **Preparation** **Phase** is mostly beneficial for **Survivors**, granting them a **40 Second** grace period without the **Killer** on the map. You, as a **Killer**, are in the **Killer Box**. You can roam around and explore it’s small interior, or use it for it’s intended purpose; **reading your abilities again before the match begins**. You can read your abilities at any time during the match, of course, by clicking ` as well though.

## **Downs & Kills**

When a Survivor's Health reaches 0, they are put into the **Downed** State and lose a tick. Upon reaching 0 ticks, if they are downed again, they will die. Alternatively, if you meet the _Requiem_ conditions for a Survivor, you can **instantly kill them.**

### **Loud Noise Notifications**

Loud Noise Notifications are special events that will cause a sudden loud sound to play for the Killer that notifies them to where a **Loud Noise** occurred, which can be triggered by several sources; Fast & Medium Vaults, Missed Skill-Checks, Rushed Locker Entries, and other miscellaneous sources.

These notifications will cause an audio-cue to play and a large icon to appear in the direction the noise came from, a large orb covering the location before dissipating. The orb only lasts for **2.5 Seconds.**

card.name: Main Goal (Attack Cooldowns, Preparation Phase, Downs, Kills, Chase, etc.)

old.desc:
# **Main Goal [Survivor]**

As a Survivor, your main goal is to stay alive and neutralize the Killer, with your secondary goals being to complete all Objectives to free all of the Survivors. You start with one of each material and your Backpack/Inventory, accessible by clicking Q while in-game.
✱ _Some maps may have alternative methods for opening the Exit._
✱ _Keybinds can be customized in the settings._

To achieve a Survivor Win, the **Killer must be neutralized** or all objectives must be completed with more than half of the Survivors escaping. Otherwise, it will end in a draw.

Objectives vary and can include Generators, Fuse Boxes, Signal Towers, Wiring, Gas Tanks, Rituals, Fog Seals, Anchors, and Exit Switches. The specifics for each Objective differ, and some are only available during certain game stages. Objective Phases advance by completing necessary Objectives displayed in the corner of your screen. Additionally, objectives will scale with player count, with the length of objectives and amount changing accordingly.

# **Preparation Phase**

At the beginning of every round, there is a **40 Second Preparation Phase**, in which all **Lights** are **disabled** and **Survivors** are tasked with mapping out their surroundings and gathering **materials**. The **Killer** is not present on the map at this time until it is over.

# **Escaping**

## **Exits**

The primary escape method is through the Exit, which will open once all main objectives have been completed. The Exit is different for each map, having unique exits and objectives. This is the alternate win condition besides neutralizing the killer.

_Escape_

- _Grants 2,500_ _**Fog Essence**_ _and +1,750_ _**Character XP**_.

## **Veiled Door**

The Veiled Door is an alternative escape available only to the last Survivor remaining alive. It spawns as an open Black Door on a random wall, shrouded in thick black fog. Survivors can enter the door by walking into it, which triggers an animation where the screen fades to black fog before transitioning to the end screen. Killers can close the door if they find it first.

_Veiled Escape_

- _Grants 4,500_ _**Fog Essence**_ _and +2,500_ _**Character XP**_.

card.name: Main Goal, Preparation Phase, & Veiled Door
Health, Healing Actions, Recovery, Ticks & Death

cards.desc:
# **Gameplay**

The game works in **Rounds**, with a walkable lobby, intermission phase, voting phase, and gameplay phase. Rounds are referred to as **Receptions.**

- _Intermission/Lobby Phase: 60 Seconds_
- _Voting Period: 25 Seconds_
- _Preparation Phase (Main Game): 30 Seconds_

In the lobby there is a **60 Second Intermission** in which players are able to roam the lobby, shop, equip items, essences, characters, and more. Once the **60 Second Intermission** is over, a **25 Second Voting Period** will begin in which players are prompted to choose from 3 randomly selected maps. _(Chosen maps will not be repeated during the next map vote, requiring a 1 Round ‘refresh’ time before it can appear again.)_

Every **3rd Round** will be followed up with an **Event Round**, adding an additional vote below the map vote allowing players to vote for the special event or gamemode to be chosen for the next round.

Once the map _(and event)_ chosen, a short cutscene featuring the next Killer will play before Survivors are placed into the map with a **40 Second Preparation Period** where the lights are out on the map and the Killers are not yet present, meant for Survivors to roam around, map out the location, and loot materials to prepare for the Survivors. After the **40 Seconds** are up, the lights are turned back on and the Killer will spawn.
Killer Spawn Locations are shown during the preparation.
Killer health bars are shown at the top of the screen and above their heads. Entity health bars will instead be only shown above their head.

This kicks off the main game-play phase, starting **The Reception** where Survivors are meant to receive and fight against the Killers, loot, craft, and gather resources to aid in their pursuits while completing map objectives to further progress the match.

Successfully vanquishing the Killer may also be referred to as _**“Curtain Calls.”**_ Assists will only grant **50% Rewards**, with the Survivor with the most damage contributed to the Killer upon death being granted **100%** of their **Curtain Call Rewards.** _(The Survivor who deals the Killing Blow will also receive 100% Rewards.)_

The **Reception** will conclude if any of the win conditions for either side are triggered.

The **Victory Screen** will display _“Curtain Call for [Killer Name] & [Killer Name] (DT Event)”_

- _This will also display in a smaller fashion in the corner (alike a Kill Feed) for all Survivors when an Entity is killed by a Survivor, stacking vertically if multiple are killed in short succession._

The **Defeat Screen** will display _“Reception of [Killer Name] & [Entity Name] (DT Event) concluded.”_
The **Draw Screen** will display _“Silence falls upon the Reception of [Killer Name] & [Killer Name] (DT Event)”_

_Survivor Win Conditions:_

- _Killer “Curtain Call” (Vanquishing the Killer(s))_
- _Escape (All Objectives Enabled & More than Half Survivors Escape)_

_Killer Win Conditions:_

- _All Survivors Dead_
- _Timer Running Out (More than Half Survivors Dead)_

_Draw Conditions:_

- _Survivors Escape with More than Half Survivors Dead_
- _Timer Runs Out with Less than Half Survivors Dead_

# **Last Man Standing**

_Last Man Standing_ is a mechanic where the last Survivor left, either through their teammates dying or leaving through the **Exit**, the last Survivor is left behind to perform a **Last Stand** against the Entities. Upon being triggered, **all Exits and Objectives are closed and locked down.**

During this, they are granted **75% Passive Resistance, +100% Power, +15% Haste,** and **Unlimited Reserve Ammo**, alongside being **Healed to Full.** They are given **3:30** **Minutes** to **vanquish all Killers** currently remaining, or simply **survive** until the timer expires.

During this, however, they are constantly **detected** by the Killers, meaning that stealth is not a choice and they **must** fight back.
cards.name: Gameplay/Rounds, LMS, & Game Modes
Rounds & Gameplay [Round System, LMS, Escape, Game Modes]

Accustomed to the fog, you now take post as a Fog Dweller, giving an exclusive title, 1.5x XP, 1.3x base Fog Essence gains, 1.5x Veiled Coins from The Well, and a 15% Price Reduction for buying Crates from Nebu.

cards.desc:
# **Status Effects**

Status Effects appear on the right side of the screen as icons, with a circular bar displaying their duration. If the Icon is **Yellow**, it is a **Buff**. If the Icon is **Red**, it's a **Debuff**.

# **Proficiency & Deficiency**

**Proficiency** is a statistic within the game that determines the speed at which you perform an action; with the base "**Proficiency**" being **1 Charge per second**. **Proficiency** can be altered depending on **Perks**, among other things. **Proficiency** is displayed in-game by the appearance of Progress Bars; with **White** being the base rate of 1c/s, **Yellow** being above 1c/s, and **Red** being below 1c/s.

**Proficiency** cannot be reduced below **0c/s.** **Proficiency** below 1c/s is referred to and considered **Deficiency.**

# **Status Effect List**

## **General Buffs**

- _**Haste**_ - % Movement Speed increase.
- _**Power**_ - % Damage increase.
- _**Concealed**_ - Conceals all actions and blocks all Aura-reading. Also applies the following effects to the effected role:
  - _(Killer)_ Hides Terror Radius/Heartbeat.
  - _(Killer)_ Hides Red Stain.
  - _(Survivor)_ Hides Shadow Trails.
- _**Silenced**_ - Silences footsteps, Loud Noise Notifications, and all ambient noises _(Ambient Killer Noises, Survivor Breathing, & Grunts of Pain)._
- _**Distortion**_ - Conceals all actions, blocks all Aura-reading, silences footsteps, Loud Noise Notifications, and all ambient noises _(Ambient Killer Noises, Survivor Breathing, & Grunts of Pain)_ as well as applying the following effects to the effected role:
  - _(Killer)_ Hides Terror Radius/Heartbeat.
  - _(Killer)_ Hides Red Stain.
  - _(Survivor)_ Hides Shadow Trails.
- _**Resistance**_ - Flat percentage damage resistance. Listed as a % when present.
- _**Overhealth**_ - Extra health applied on top of current HP. No special effects.

### **Survivor Only**

Buffs that only apply to Survivors.

- _**Endurance**_ - Negates all damage taken when hit and instead afflicts the Survivor with _Fatal Wounds_ and removes a stack of this status. If the attack does not deal over **20 Damage**, only Negate the damage and do not proc _Fatal Wounds_. Can be quantified in a duration timer or in stacks, stacks lasting until the on-hit effect is proc’d. _On-Hit Haste is still applied when hit by any ability that procs it._
- _**Grace**_ - Negates all damage taken when hit, then remove a stack of this Status. Can be quantified in a duration timer or in stacks, stacks lasting until the on-hit effect is proc’d. _On-Hit Haste is still applied when hit by any ability that procs it._

### **Killer Only**

Buffs that only apply to Killers.

- _**Enraged**_ - +5% Haste and Immunity to all damage and stuns.

## **General Ailments**

Universal Ailments.

- _**Hinder/Hindered**_ - % Movement Speed reduction.
- _**Stunned**_ - Immobilizes and disables the target entirely; disallowing them from moving, attacking, or otherwise using an ability.
- _**Blindness**_ - Disables the ability to see **auras** by any mean.
- _**Fragility**_ - Targets take X% more damage from all sources except **Status Effects**.
- _**Weakness**_ - Targets deal X% less damage.
- _**Broken**_ - Targets suffering from Broken cannot be healed or heal themselves by any means, including **Perks**.
- _**Darkness**_ - Targets suffering from Darkness suffer from Oblivious, have their vision set to 30 Studs, and only see a pitch black darkness past that range.
- _**Helpless**_ - Disables all ability use.
- _**Crippled**_ - Disables Sprinting and Stamina regeneration.
- _**Deafened -**_ Audio is muffled and a distinct tinnitus-like ringing sound plays over most external audio.
- _**Dazed -**_ Vision becomes indistinct and blurred, causing the camera to wobble randomly back and forth.
- _**Nausea**_ - Works similarly to **Dazed**, but adds a nasty hue and more intense blurring and vision distortion. The victim is trapped into a keeling animation once the status expires, bending over and holding their stomach.

## **General DOT Effects**

Universal DOT effects. If the duration of these status' exceed **15 Seconds**, halve the duration and increase the status tier by one. Max Tier is **Tier V.** Any tier application of any of these status' will add to/reset the current duration, regardless of the current tier. If the status adds to a duration, it will add the duration it inflicts onto the current duration. However, if it simply applies a new instance of the status, it will increase the tier of the status. If the current duration is less than **6 Seconds** when it’s tier is increased by a new instance, it will add **half** the duration of the new instance to the current duration.

- _**Bleed**_ - Targets suffering from **Bleed** will take **1 Damage** per second. For each tier above one, will halve the amount of time between each tick**.** At **Tier III+**, double the damage of this status. Does not damage at or below **10 HP.**
  - At **Tier V**, if the duration of this status exceeds **15 Seconds**, evolve into **Hemorrhaged** at equal duration.
- _**Burn -**_ Targets suffering from **Burn** will take **2 Damage/s.** Every tier above 1 will deal **+1 Damage/s.** Can deal **Fatal Damage.**
- _**Poison**_ - Targets suffering from **Poison** will take **1 Damage/0.5s** and will suppress all forms of healing. Each tick of Poison will inflict **+1% Fragility** for **+1 Second.** Every tier above one will deal increase **Fragility Limit** by **5%.** Cannot deal **Fatal Damage.**
  - At **Tier III+**, cause **Nausea.**
  - **Base Max Fragility** application is **10%**, capped at **35%** at **Tier V.**
- _**Hemorrhaged**_ - Targets suffering from **Hemorrhaged** take **1% MaxHP/s** and suppress all forms of healing. The portion of health removed by **Hemorrhaged** appears as a striped, deep red and darkened segment of the player's health bar. After expiring, inflict **Broken** equal to the duration of **Hemorrhaged.** Can deal **Fatal Damage.**
  - Bleed cannot be applied at the same time as Hemorrhaged. Bleed application will simply add to the duration of Hemorrhaged.

### **Killer Only Ailments**

Ailments that only effect Killers.

- _W.I.P_

### **Survivor Only Ailments**

Ailments that only effect Survivors.

- _**Exhausted**_ - Disables the use of Exhaustion Perks. While Exhausted, Survivors will pant heavily.
- _**Stagger** -_ Stagger is caused when a **Survivor** either falls from a great height or is afflicted by an **Ability**. Upon being afflicted with **Stagger**, **Survivor Movement Speed** is set to **0**. After **25%** of the **Stagger Duration** has elapsed, **Survivors** will regain their speed over a linear period of time, which is fully regained once the **Stagger Duration** has fully elapsed.
- _**Peril**_ - Survivors are instantly put into the **Dying State** upon being hit by a **Standard Attack** regardless of **Health**.
- _**Mangled**_ - Survivors suffering from **Mangled** will bleed profusely, leaving much more blood behind them. Healing progress will regress at **300%** the normal rate.
- _**Open Wounds**_ - Can stack up to three times _(Stacks have no additional effects)_. Increases the Charges required to heal by **25%**, and also decreasing healing speeds by **25%** for one Heal. After healing or the duration expires, remove this status. _(Applies to healing self or being healed by others)_
  - If Open Wounds is applied thrice, turn this status into **Deep Wounds.**
- _**Deep Wounds**_ - Can stack up to two times _(Stacks have no additional effects)_. Increases the Charges required to heal by **25%**, and also decreasing healing speeds by **25%** for one Heal. _(Applies to healing self or being healed by others)_ Any time the victim uses an ability while afflicted by Deep Wounds, apply **Hemorrhaging** to them for **3 Seconds.**
  - If Deep Wounds is applied twice, turn this status into **Fatal Wounds.**
- _**Fatal Wounds**_ - Starts a **15 Second** timer that will regress while not running. Once the **15 Second** timer is up, the **Survivor** afflicted will be downed. **Fatal Wounds** can be cleansed by performing a **Mend Action** on yourself or have another **Survivor** mend you.
  - Mending takes **8 Charges** to complete, and will incur the self-healing speed penalty.
  - After mending, the victim will gain **Deep Wounds.**
- _**Oblivious -**_ Cannot hear Killer Heartbeat or Terror Radius.
- _**Hexed**_ - Survivors suffering from Hexed are currently affected by an active Sigil and it's effects.
  - Some **Sigil Effects** will be hidden until a certain requirement is met or stay hidden.
- _**Dread**_ - Survivor is afflicted with **Oblivious** and **Blindness.** Chase music is disabled, replaced by a constant full **Heartbeat** sound _(without the icon)_. Vision is severely impaired, with a range restricted to **35 Studs**, a pronounced vignette effect around the border of the screen, and a slightly weaker blindness affliction. The Survivor also hears constant sounds of distant screams surrounding them.
- _**Incapacitated**_ - Unable to interact with anything except for Vaults and Wedges.

### **Survivor Character-Exclusive Ailments**

Ailments that are used exclusively by a certain Survivor Character.

- _**Scorn**_ - Status exclusive to [**Virgil**](https://trello.com/c/rCKTPpuV/137-virgil "‌")**.** Targets suffering from **Scorn** will take **X%** of **Current HP** as damage after **10 Seconds.** This has a special effect as it grows closer to bursting, with a ring of ash revolving around them, growing larger and brighter before reaching a critical point with a glowing orange effect before bursting into an explosion of slag and fire. _(x = stack count)_
- _**Achromat**_ - Status exclusive to [**Alexander Glynn II**](https://trello.com/c/fZuSQPCH/136-alexander-glynn-ii "‌")**.** Stacking status, up to **7.** Stacks can be removed by downing Survivors. The following effects will take place at certain stack amounts:
  - **1+ Stacks:** Apply a light vignette filter and darken vision, saturating and reducing the brightness of all colors. Increases in strength the more stacks that are applied.
  - **2+ Stacks:** Using an ability will **Seal** it for **6 Seconds**, restricting usage and not allowing it’s cooldown to count down while sealed.
  - **3+ Stacks:** Incur a passive **10% Fragility.**
  - **4+ Stacks:** **Seal** duration is increased to **10 Seconds.**
  - **5+ Stacks:** **Nullify** all sources of **Distortion.**
  - **6+ Stacks:** **Stuns** and all **negative status effects** have their duration increased by **50%** and reduce the duration of **positive effects** by **50%.** Now nullify all sources of **Haste, Silenced,** and **Distortion.**
  - **7 Stacks:** Take **10% Max HP** as damage, **Stun** self, gain **Deafened**, and incur the **unique blindness** from **Starstruck** for **5 Seconds**, then remove all stacks.

### **Survivor Character-Exclusive DOT Effects**

DOT Effects that are used exclusively by a certain Survivor Character.

- hi :3

### **Killer Character-Exclusive Ailments**

Ailments that are used exclusively by a certain Killer Character.

- _**Oozing**_ - [Drowned](https://trello.com/c/k2cjpmRK/409-the-drowned "‌") Exclusive Status Effect. Causes a dripping aura of a dark, blue liquid. Incurs a **-5%** **Hinder** and causes **Survivors** to leave a constant pool of the blue liquid as they move. An oozing screen effect will cover the **Survivor’s** screen
- _**Depth Virus**_ - [Drowned](https://trello.com/c/k2cjpmRK/409-the-drowned "‌") Exclusive Status Effect. Survivors who die with **Depth Virus** will be transformed into a [**Wretch**](https://trello.com/c/Nk8hmS1O/144-wretch "‌").
- _**Dreaming**_ - [Nightmare](https://trello.com/c/EtD8bPii/411-the-nightmare "‌") Exclusive Status Effect. **Oblivious** while afflicted, and occasionally fake **Doors** and **Tools** will appear. Take **10 Extra Damage** upon being hit by a **Standard Attack** while afflicted with **Dreaming**. The **Survivors** worldview is changed as well, becoming darker and dream-like. The Dreaming State will also make you vulnerable to some of [Nightmare](https://trello.com/c/3F2Cd1GD/37-the-nightmare-skins-lore-wip "‌")’s abilities. The **Dreaming State** is permanent until the **Survivor** until they are either hit by a **Standard Attack**, placed into a **Foghold**, miss a **Skill-Check** while performing an **Objective**, or finish performing a **Dreamer’s Rite**.
- _**Parasomnia**_ - [Nightmare](https://trello.com/c/EtD8bPii/411-the-nightmare "‌") Exclusive Status Effect. **Oblivious** while afflicted, always max Heartbeat, vision restricted to **30 Studs**, as well as seeing visions of shadows charging towards them and screaming at them.
- _**Hysteria -**_ [Banshee](https://trello.com/c/qwDPSGFV/32-the-banshee "‌") Exclusive Status Effect. Survivor is **Incapacitated**, constantly suffers from a maximum **Heartbeat**, and their **camera wobbles** slightly. A thick **vignette** filter appears around the borders of the screen. Additionally, the Survivor suffers from occasional **directional drift**, causing movement inputs to have a chance to be slightly **offset** and temporarily pushed in a random direction.

### **Killer Character-Exclusive DOT Effects**

DOT Effects that are used exclusively by a certain Killer Character.

- hi :3

# **Lobby Inventory**

In the lobby, there is a Backpack icon that stores all bought **Items &** [**Catalysts**](https://trello.com/c/CgQpmzPf/282-refractions-catalysts "‌") from Nebu, as well as Stat Multipliers obtained from **Codes** or **In-Game Purchases**.

# **Character Menu**

The **Character Menu** is a **Menu** that can be accessed to inspect all aspects of the **Character** you opened. This **Menu** contains the **Character’s** **Abilities, Perks, Level,** and **Prestige**.

cards.desc:
# **Kit Difficulty Key**

## **1 - Beginner**

Characters rated as Beginner are straightforward to use and ideal for new players. Their abilities are easy to understand and use, with a forgiving learning curve. These Characters offer solid performance without requiring extensive knowledge of game mechanics.

## **2 - Intermediate**

Intermediate Characters require a moderate understanding of the game’s mechanics. Players need to have a good grasp of basic strategies and game knowledge to use these Characters effectively. They may have abilities that require some proper coordination and skill to get value from, making them suitable for players who have moved past the basics.

## **3 - Advanced**

Advanced Characters demand a higher level of skill and strategic thinking. Their abilities might have more complex interactions and require a much higher amount of skill to use. Players need to understand the nuances of the game and be able to effectively use these Characters, as well as maximize their potential in-game.

## **4 - Expert**

Expert Characters are challenging to master and require a deep understanding of the game’s mechanics. They often have powerful abilities that need precise timing and/or positioning. Players must have excellent situational awareness and the ability to appropriately read the game to maximize effectiveness.

## **5 - Master**

Master Characters are very difficult to use but can be extremely rewarding in the hands of a skilled player. They have intricate kits with high potential for strategic play. Mastering these Characters requires extensive practice, exceptional game knowledge, and the ability to adapt to any situation. Players must excel in all aspects of gameplay, including strategy, timing, positioning, and anticipating opponents' moves. These characters are designed for those who seek the highest level of challenge and are capable of taking advantage of every aspect of their kit to its fullest potential.

## **6 - Grand-Master**

Grand-Master Characters are the epitome of difficulty, offering extremely intricate mechanics within how they function and must be played correctly to truly utilize the Character’s kit. This Echelon of Character is defined by it’s mechanics and abilities; having every requirement of Master Difficulty Characters and more.
cards.name: Kit Difficulty Key

cards.desc:
# **Rounds**

The game works in **Rounds**, with a walkable lobby, intermission phase, voting phase, and gameplay phase.

- _Intermission/Lobby Phase: 60 Seconds_
- _Voting Period: 25 Seconds_
- _Preparation Phase (Main Game): 40 Seconds_
- _Beginning Round Timer: 12:30 Minutes_
- _Rift Anchors Enable: 3:00 Minutes Remaining_

In the lobby there is a **60 Second Intermission** in which players are able to roam the lobby, shop, equip items, perks, characters, and more. Once the **60 Second Intermission** is over, a **25 Second Voting Period** will begin in which players are prompted to choose from 3 randomly selected maps. Once the map is chosen, a short cutscene featuring the next Killer Character will play before Survivors are placed into the map with a **40 Second Preparation Period** where the lights are out on the map and the Killer is not yet present, meant for Survivors to roam around, map out the location, and loot materials to prepare for the Killer. After the **40 Seconds** is up, the lights are turned back on and the Killer will emerge.

This kicks off the main game-play phase, where Survivors are meant to fight against the Killer, loot, craft, and gather resources to aid in their pursuits while completing map objectives to further progress and turn on Rift Anchors to grant them more time.

The round will end if any of the win conditions for either side are triggered.

_Survivor Win Conditions:_

- _Killer Neutralization (Killing the Killer)_
- _Escape (All Objectives Enabled & More than Half Survivors Escape)_

_Killer Win Conditions:_

- _All Survivors Dead_
- _Timer Running Out (More than Half Survivors Dead)_

_Draw Conditions:_

- _Survivors Escape with More than Half Survivors Dead_
- _Timer Runs Out with Less than Half Survivors Dead_
cards.name: Rounds

cards.desc:
# **Currency**

## _Fog Essence_

An ethereal energy that one can absorb to become more intertwined with the Fog, vitalising themselves and manifesting items to aid with manoeuvring within the Fog. Despite being trapped here by the Fog, one must rely on it.
First Main currency, able to be used to upgrade **Characters**.
**Primarily obtained from winning matches.**
Caps off at 10,000,000.

## _Veiled Coins_

Mysterious coins with markings etched all throughout them. They radiate an ominous presence, clearly holding deep power within. They seem to be offerings of sorts.
Second Main currency. Can be used to unlock characters, purchase perks from the shop, or other miscellaneous goods.
**Primarily Obtained from Levelling Up, Achievements, Daily/Weekly Rituals & Events/Codes.**
Has no cap.

## _Fog Shards_

Arcane shards of fog so dense that it has taken form. They give off ominous energy, with dark fog seeping deep from within it it.
The paid currency that can be used to buy various things, such as characters or other miscellaneous goods.
**Exclusively Obtained from Paying Robux and buying Chapters, and rarely Giveaways & Events/Codes.**
Has no cap.
cards.name: Currency

cards.desc:
# _Player Leveling_

Players too have a Level, which doesn't weigh on game-play that much besides showing how dedicated you are to the game. Much simpler than _Character Leveling_, **Player Leveling** will cost **10,000 XP** to level up. Every **25 Levels**, the **XP Requirement** will increase by **10,000XP**. Leveling up will reward the player with **100 Veiled Coins** every time.
You gain XP dependent on your performance in-game, as well; with XP rewards listed below.

- _1XP every second spent alive in-game. Caps off at 900. (15 Minutes)_
- _0XP for each Medal of_ **Ashen Quality**
- _400XP for each Medal of_ **Dissonant Quality**
- _900XP for each Medal of_ **Luminous Quality**
- _2,025XP for each Medal of_ **Radiant Quality**
- _4,555XP for each Medal of_ **Ethereal Quality**
- _10,000XP for each Medal of_ **Veiled Quality**
- _750XP for the_ **First Game of the Day** _(Caps at 1,500, for playing both Survivor & Killer)_

# _Medals_

**Medals**, as mentioned above, are tied to your performance in-game. The **Scores** directly correspond to the **Fog Essence** you will earn at the end of the match, being 1:1; meaning your **Score** is the reward you will get. **Catalysts** can effect this, but will only directly boost the **reward** you will get at the **end** of the match and not the amount of **Score** you gain **during** the match.
The **Fog Essence** cap for **Survivor** is **17,500**, while the cap for **Killers** is **25,000**. Obtaining a **Ethereal Quality Medal** will grant you an extra **25 Veiled Coins** on top of your standard **Fog Essence** reward, and getting a **Veiled Quality Medal** will give you **100 Veiled Coins** as well.
**Medals** are granted for each **Score** in-game, _Objectives, Survival, Altruism, and Boldness_ for **Survivor** and _Domination, Malice, Pursuit, and Execution_ for **Killer**. Each **Score** in these categories will award a corresponding **Medal**. **Medal Quality** requirements are as follows (_First Number is for Survivor, second is for Killer_);

- _Ashen: 2,500/4,750 and Below in any Score_
- _Dissonant: 4,750/7,500+ in any Score_
- _Luminous: 7,500/9,875+ in any Score_
- _Radiant: 9,875/13,150+ in any Score_
- _Ethereal: 13,150/17,500+ in any Score_
- _Veiled: Fog Essence Cap in any Score_

# _Devotion_

**Devotion** is another type of Level that Players can access once they reach their Level Cap. The Level Cap starts at Level 50. Upon reaching their Level Cap, Players can pay Veiled Coins to increase their **Devotion Tier.** For each **Devotion Tier**, the Level Cap will increase by +25 Max Levels and Players will receive a +10% Boost to _**all**_ **Fog Essence** and **Veiled Coin** gain. Some things in the game may also require a **Devotion Tier** to access.
cards.name: Player Leveling, Score System/Round Rewards, Medals, and Devotion

cards.desc:
# _**Character Leveling**_

**Character Leveling** is defined by the individual **Character's Level**, such as any given **Killer** or any given **Survivor Character**. Character's can be leveled up to Level 50, in which the **Player** can pay **Fog Essence** to **Prestige** the Character. **Players** may also pay **Fog Essence** to speed up the **Level Progression** of the Characters, or earn **XP** by playing matches with the aforementioned Character. The **XP** is earned as a spendable **Currency** for the **Character** you are playing with. The **XP** is earned at a 1:5 ratio of XP to FE, as stated below this. The **XP** (_And Fog Essence_) can be spent in something called the **Constellation**. You can earn as much excess **XP** as you want, and it will also carry over when **Prestiging**.

# _**Constellation**_

The **Constellation** is the way in which you level up your **Characters**. When opening the **Constellation**, it will open a full-screen UI display a vast **Galaxy** with many stars glinting in and out of the background, with a connected series of boxes with many interconnections between each. Each box is called a **Star Node**, and each **Star Node** features one of the following items; **Item** (_Survivor Only, Only Tools_), **Refraction/Item Refraction** (_Item Refraction Survivor Only_), **Perk**, or **Catalyst**. Each **Star Node** will have a bright, shining star behind it. Every **Constellation Level** will randomly generate **Star Nodes**, which will have randomised items in each of the nodes.
**Level 0** **Constellations** will feature a total of **5 Star Nodes**. The number of **Star Nodes** is determined by the current **Constellation Level**. Each **Level** will add **1 Star Node** per level. Every **5 Constellation Levels** will feature a **huge** **Constellation**, being **5 Star Nodes** more than the current **Level**. The max number of **Star Nodes** that can appear is reached at **Constellation Level 25**; with **25 Star Nodes**. However, the extra **Star Nodes** that spawn per **5 Levels** ignores this cap. **Constellation Level 50**, the **Max Level**, features one, singular large **Star Node** in the middle which will prompt the text; “**Are you certain you want to ascend to [Prestige X]?**” Upon confirming, a bright light will consume the screen before the **Prestige Number** of the **Character** goes up by one and the **Constellation Level** is reset.

**Star Node Shapes**:

- _Item - Rounded Square_
- _Refraction - Rounded Square w/ Star on the top right Corner_
- _Perk - Square Rotated 45°_ _and Encased_
- _Catalyst - Hexagon_
  ![Sprite-0001.jpg](https://trello.com/1/cards/6620278a9fe039db1c6f415e/attachments/66ff68e56f41132cb5dbbbcf/download/Sprite-0001.jpg)

_Ashen, Dissonant,_ and _Luminous_ rarity **Star Nodes** will spawn from **Level 0**. After **Level 10**, _Radiant_ **Star Nodes** will begin spawning. After **Level 20**, _Ethereal_ **Star Nodes** begin spawning. After **Level 30,** _Veiled_ **Star Nodes** begin spawning. At least **two** _Radiant_ **Star Nodes** will spawn per **Level** after **Level 20**. At least **one** _Ethereal_ **Star Node** will spawn per **Level** after **Level 30.**
**Tier I Perks** are **Dissonant Rarity**, **Tier II Perks** are **Luminous Rarity**, and **Tier III Perks** are **Radiant Rarity**. _Common Tool = Ashen Rarity Price, Uncommon Tool = Dissonant Rarity Price, Rare Tool = Luminous Rarity Price, Epic Tool = Radiant Rarity Price. Legendary Tool = Ethereal Rarity Price._

**Star Node Rarity Prices:**

- _Ashen - 800XP_
- _Dissonant - 850XP_
- _Luminous - 1,600XP_
- _Radiant - 3,350XP_
- _Ethereal - 4,350XP_
- _Veiled - 5,000XP_

**Star Node Rarity Chances:**

- _Ashen - 36%_
- _Dissonant - 24%_
- _Luminous - 16%_
- _Radiant - 12%_
- _Ethereal - 8%_
- _Veiled - 4%_

**Star Node Rarity Caps:**

- _Ashen - No Cap._
- _Dissonant - No Cap._
- _Luminous - No Cap._
- _Radiant - 8 Nodes._
- _Ethereal - 4 Nodes._
- _Veiled - 1 Node._

### _**Star Variability**_

Once **Constellation Level 10** has been reached, **Star Variability** begins. Once the fourth **Star Node** has been purchased, a foggy **Star Node** will spawn outside of the interconnected **Star Nodes**. Once the fifth **Star Node** has been purchased, it will connect to the outermost and closest **Star Node** and dim it out, rendering it inaccessible. It will then dim out one connected **Star Node** from any of the connected **Star Nodes** it has dimmed with each consecutive purchase of a **Star Node**. However, the **Star Variability** can begin early if the **Player** chooses to purchase a **Perk Node**, causing **Star Variability** to begin early on and specifically dim one of the other **Perk Nodes**.

- **Star Variability** will favor dimming the highest rarity **Star Node** if it can, but will otherwise continue on it’s current path and only dim directly adjacent **Nodes**.
- _Abridged: Once the fifth **Star Node** is purchased, one of the outermost **Star Nodes** will become unpurchasable, and for each **Star Node** purchased afterwards one more **Star Node** is dimmed until the entire **Constellation Level** is completed. This loop will continue for each and every **Level** after **10**, up until **Level 50**._

Once **Prestiged**, the **Star Variability** will disable again until **Level 10** has been reached again.

# _**Prestige**_

**Prestige** is a mechanic in which you can reset a Character's Level back down to 0, but gain a reward. For **Prestige** I - III, you will unlock all 3 of the Character's Perks at the corresponding Tier for **all** Characters. This means that you can use that Characters Perks on any Character of the role with the Tier of which **Prestige** is reached. (_Prestige I - Tier I, Prestige II - Tier II, Prestige III - Tier III_)
At **Prestige III**, you also unlock two exclusive cosmetics for the **Character**; a Icon and Banner. At **Prestige C** (_100_), you will unlock an exclusive **Veiled Rarity Skin** for the **Character**.

# _**Mastery**_

**Mastery** is obtained by completing "**Mastery Achievements**," which are **Achievements** related to that specific Character. When **Mastery** is attained, you unlock that Character's **Mastery Perk** and **Mastery Skin**.
cards.name: Character Leveling, Constellation, Prestige, and Mastery

cards.desc:
###### Prices here are not permanent and may, and likely will, change in the future.

# **Nebueil**

## _"Got some rare things on sale, stranger!"_

Nebueil is the excitable and expressive shopkeeper in the rift, providing all kinds of goods and services. Located at one side of the lobby, always behind the counter.

Nebu appears as a dog-like black shape inside of a stack of CRT TV’s sitting behind the counter, a mysterious pitch black fog emanating from behind the TVs. Nebu’s head appears in the top TV, moving accordingly as they speak. Nebu will also occasionally move between the TVs, showing their arms and hands when called for. Nebu has no other discernible features.
The TVs glitch out occasionally, showing static.

While in their shop, the song [Resurrections](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1rwAvUvvQzQ&ab_channel=LenaRaine "‌") by Lena Raine will play.

## _**Nebu's Wares**_

Menu in which you can find special items and perks Nebu is selling.
Four random perks will be in stock, changing every week; being at a base price of **2,000 Veiled Coins** at Tier I, **4,000** at Tier II, and **6,000** at Tier III.
There will also be a scroll-able menu on the other side of the screen to buy **Weapons & Tools** to spawn in with for the next match.

## _**Skin Crates**_

Skin Crates that can be bought with Veiled Coins. They come in two types, Tool Crates and Killer Crates; each coming in three rarities as well: Rare, Epic, and Legendary.
Tool Crates can give skins for Flash-lights, Backpacks, Toolboxes, and all Guns.
Killer Crates give skins for.. Killers. You know.. the name of it.

### **Rarity Chances**

- _Ethereal Pity: 125 Crates_
- _Dissonant Crate Price: 250 Veiled Coins / 15 Rift Shards_
- _Dissonant Crate Rates:_
  - _Dissonant - 74.5%_
  - _Luminous - 20%_
  - _Radiant - 5%_
  - _Ethereal - 0.5%_
- _Ethereal Pity: 40 Crates_
- _Luminous Crate Price: 750 Veiled Coins / 50 Rift Shards_
- _Luminous Crate Rates:_
  - _Luminous - 88%_
  - _Radiant - 10%_
  - _Ethereal - 2%_
- _Ethereal Pity: 6 Crates_
- _Radiant Crate Price: 2,250 Veiled Coins / 100 Rift Shards_
- _Radiant Crate Rates:_
  - _Radiant - 90%_
  - _Ethereal - 10%_

### **Killer Skin Rarity Prices**

**Killer** skins can also be bought individually, with the prices for each **Rarity** being;

- **Ashen - 375 Veiled Coins**
- **Dissonant - 900 Veiled Coins**
- **Luminous - 1,750 Veiled Coins**
- **Radiant - 3,250 Veiled Coins**
- **Ethereal - 5,000 Veiled Coins**
- **Veiled - Cannot be bought.**

## **Survivor Skins**

**Survivor** skins can only be bought individually, with the prices for each **Rarity** being;

- **Ashen - 250 Veiled Coins**
- **Dissonant - 750 Veiled Coins**
- **Luminous - 1,550 Veiled Coins**
- **Radiant - 2,500 Veiled Coins**
- **Ethereal - 3,500 Veiled Coins**
- **Veiled - Cannot be bought.**

## _**Chapters of the Rift**_

## _**Rift's Goods**_

Menu in which you can find purchasable currency and [Gamepasses](https://trello.com/c/5FlOFzdS/20-gamepasses "‌").

### **Currency Offers**

_Fog Shards_

- _50 Fog Shards (R$50)_
- _265 Fog Shards (R$250) [+5%~ Bonus]_
- _550 Fog Shards (R$500) [+10% Bonus]_
- _850 Fog Shards (R$750) [+12.5%~ Bonus]_
- _1,150 Fog Shards (R$1,000) [+15% Bonus]_
- _3,125 Fog Shards (R$2,500) [+25% Bonus]_
- _7,500 Fog Shards (R$5,000) [+50% Bonus]_

## _**Tale's of the Rift**_

Clickable button that will have Nebu give comments about The Fog, or _The Dark_.

## _**Whispers (Codes)**_

Menu in which you can input [Codes](https://trello.com/c/lnkwv9Ev/92-codes "‌") for different rewards.

cards.desc:
# **Last Man Standing**

A large flavor text will appear, displaying the text “Last Man Standing.” During this, the timer will appear as _“???”_ and an ominous ticking sound will play in the background. The timer is changed to **5:00 Minutes** during this.

The last Survivor and the Killer will **not** be shown to each other, unless the Survivor does not perform any actions such as interacting with an objective or attack the Killer _(mutually exclusive)_ for over **60 Seconds**, in which the Survivor will be **highlighted** for **5 Seconds** and a **Loud Noise Notification** will play at their location.

This serves as the very last stand, with the last Survivor either finishing the job in neutralizing the Killer, completing the last objectives and escaping, or finding the Veiled Door and escaping, or the Killer ending the hunt and finishing off the last Survivor.
cards.name: Last Man Standing

cards.desc:
# **Catalysts & Refractions**

# **Catalysts**

**Catalysts** are a special type of **Item** that **Players** can equip onto their **Character** (_If obtained in the Character’s_ [_Constellation_](https://trello.com/c/tBRdYwfm/28-character-leveling-prestige-and-mastery "‌")) or to their **Player** from their [**Lobby Inventory**](https://trello.com/c/Pv4c77oT/95-lobby-inventory-character-menu "‌").

When a round begins, a screen plays in which all of the offered **Catalysts** will be burnt; one by one, and in a random order, including the **Killer’s.**

**Catalysts** come in **all** Rarities, from _Ashen_ to _Veiled_ with a wide range of effects to the next match. After the match is over, the **Catalyst** will shatter and be removed from your possession. The types of **Catalysts** are as follows;

- _Map Catalysts_; will increase the chance of being sent to a specific map. Can be nullified and guarantee a completely random map if a special type of Map Catalyst (_“Arcane Map Catalyst“_) is brought. **Map Catalysts** only come in the **Luminous** **Rarity**.
- _Fog’s Favour Catalysts_; Alters certain aspects of the match, such as the [_Veiled Door_](https://trello.com/c/TrxVYsIa/98-veiled-door "‌") spawn location or where Survivors spawn.
- _Fog Essence Catalysts_; will increase the amount of **Fog Essence** earned at the end of the match. Lower **Rarity** variants of this **Catalyst** will be restricted to increasing only the amount of **Fog Essence** gained in a specific category for you, while **Catalysts** of **Radiant+** **Quality** will grant _everyone_ boosted **Fog Essence** in those categories. **Ethereal** **Catalysts** of this type will grant **every Player** in the match boosted **Fog Essence**. (_Fog Essence Catalysts do stack, and are additive. Not multiplicative._)
- _Requiem Catalysts_; **Killer-Exclusive** **Catalyst** that allows the **Killer** to **kill** **Survivors** without **Banishing** them. The different rarities give different benefits and effects to this **Catalyst**.
- _Loot Catalysts_; Manipulates the way Crates & Chests spawn.

### **Secret Catalysts**

Secret Catalysts are Catalysts that aren’t shown during the **Catalyst Burning** screen.

## **List of Catalysts**

#### _**Map Catalyst List**_

All _Map Catalysts_ are of the **Luminous** rarity and are shared general Catalysts with no separated items for each role.

  - Increases the likelihood of being sent to a Map in the [MV Elysium](https://trello.com/c/4EprKhFQ/163-mv-elysium "‌") Realm.
- _**Pale Twig**_ _- “A twig from the pale forest.“_
  - Increases the likelihood of being sent to a Map in the [Limbo](https://trello.com/c/wDp8XXrj/164-limbo "‌") Realm.
- _**Painted Stone**_ _- “A throwing stone painted by a child of the region surrounding Lake Vaikal._
  - Increases the likelihood of being sent to a Map in the [Sinevo Village](https://trello.com/c/a5NM4OyZ/273-village-lake-vaikal "‌") Realm.
- _**Royal Decree**_ _- “A stone envelope, cracks and fissures lining it’s body; once containing a letter of royal authority.“_
  - Increases the likelihood of being sent to a Map in the [Kingdom of Sorrow](https://trello.com/c/DNZy16wo/377-kingdom-of-sorrow "‌") Realm.

# **Refractions**

**Refractions** are a special type of item that directly change the behaviour of certain things depending on the role it’s used on. If equipped on **Survivor**, it will change the behaviour of **Items, Weapons, & Tools**. If equipped on **Killer**, it will directly change the behaviour of the **Killer’s Powers.**

**Refractions** are going to be directly found inside of each **Character’s** [**Character Menu**](https://trello.com/c/Pv4c77oT/95-lobby-inventory-character-menu "‌") and by going to the ‘**Refractions’** section. In this section, if for a **Survivor**; the **Refractions** are sorted firstly by the **Item** they effect and also by **Rarity**. If on **Killer**; the **Refractions** are sorted solely by **Rarity**. Hovering over each **Refraction** will explain what it does. You can equip up to **Four** **Refractions** on **Survivor** and **Two Refractions** on **Killer**.

**Survivor Refractions** can be found [**here**](https://trello.com/c/0rTK5VMd/283-survivor-refractions "‌").
**Killer Refractions** will be listed in the **Killer’s Card/Description** directly under _**“Loadout.”**_
cards.name: Catalysts & Refractions

cards.desc:
# **Daily/Weekly Rituals**

**Rituals** are this game’s system of Daily and Weekly Quests, granting rewards for completing them.

Players will get **3 Daily Rituals** and **3 Weekly Rituals** for each role at the named intervals.

There are **two** types of Rituals, being **Daily** and **Weekly.** There are further divided by **Survivor Rituals** and **Killer Rituals**, which can be switched between in the **Ritual Menu.**

The different types of Rituals have varying rewards of **Veiled Coins**, with Daily Rituals giving far less than Weeklies but will **refresh daily**, allowing for a viable way of grinding Veiled Coins besides leveling.

There are also unique Rituals which will have you play with **randomised load-outs** as well, and all of these different Rituals will have varying rewards depending on how difficult they are.

## **Survivor Ritual Components**

Survivor Rituals will mainly consist of **Performing Objectives** such as completing **Generators,** **Misc. Objectives**, or interacting with **Sigils** and **Glyphs** and/or winning as a specific **Survivor Character** or attaining Survivor Victories in general.

## **Killer Ritual Components**

Killer Rituals will mainly consist of **Getting Kills or Wins** with specific Killers or in general.

# **Daily Ritual List**

This list will be updated as I add more.

Dailies are reset every day at 12:00 CST.

## **Survivor**

### **General**

- _**“The Masquerade”**_
  - Escape with [Random Owned Survivor] using [Random Loadout (Perks & Refractions)] _(Interactable to Equip)_
    - _500 Veiled Coins_
- _**“The Light”**_
  - Escape 1~10 Times
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“Light in the Dark”**_
  - Complete 5~10 Full Generators
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“Initiate”**_
  - Perform Cooperative Actions for a total of 120~240 Seconds
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“The Courage”**_
  - Stun the Killer 1~7 Times
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“The Fearlessness”**_
  - Deal a total of 1,250~2,500 Damage _(250 per Interval)_
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“The Faithful”**_
  - Heal other Survivors 5~25 Times _(5 per Interval)_
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“The Will”**_
  - Engage in a Chase for a total of 120~240 Seconds
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_
- _**“The Expectation”**_
  - Open the Exit Gate 1~3 Times
    - _12,500~25,000 Fog Essence_

cards.desc:
# **Perks**

Perks are a core part of game-play, giving both sides massive boosts and abilities. Some Perks are special, such as those listed for the [**Survivors**](https://trello.com/c/sausxNbD/122-glyph-ritual-and-rune-perks "‌") and [**Killers**](https://trello.com/c/ydjNDlVx/123-sigil-perks "‌").

## **Perk System**

Both **Killers** and **Survivors** are given **15 Perk Points,** and **7 Perk Slots.** Each **Perk** has a **Point Cost** from **1-5 Points,** which will spend **Perk Points** to equip.

The Point Cost is determined by the impact of the Perk, with low impact Perks costing less and higher impact Perks costing more.

Mastery Perks have their own dedicated slot that only Mastery Perks can be equipped into, and only _one_ Mastery Perk can be equipped into it.

_Perk Points are refunded when unequipping a Perk._

## **Shared/Character Perks**

Shared/Character Perks are Perks that are unlocked for other Characters after Prestiging a Character. When you Prestige a Character, it will unlock their 3 Exclusive Perks to be used on any other Characters. These Perks still need to be tiered up, however they will always be equal in Tier to the Character's Prestige (the one who they come from) up to Tier III, in which they will be Tier III for all Characters.
These Perks are unlocked at Tier I on their Character upon unlocking the Character.

## **Mastery Perks**

Mastery Perks are a special type of Perk that is unlocked upon achieving Mastery on a Character. These Perks do not have Tiers and have a gold rim on their Icon. These are different from Shared Perks, in the nature that they cannot be used until Mastery has been achieved, while Shared/Character Perks are always usable by their respective Character.
cards.name: Perks & Perk System

cards.desc:
# **Rifts**

Rifts are small areas of any map in which **The Fog** has seeped in, composing of darkened and foggier sections that mimic the rest of the map in style; aside from it being consumed by a dark mass; transforming it into an inky black area. These areas grant a unique addition to game-play, allowing players to walk into a monolith located in the center, transporting them to a random location on the map, seen emerging through a wall; accessible in a sub-level of the rifts, while also giving **Survivors** a reliable **loop** to work with as well. These can be accessed by walking down a set of black stairs somewhere in the rift. One **Chest** and one **Crate** are guaranteed to spawn in The Ambry at the beginning of the match.

The ‘portals’ opened by walking into the monolith will stay open for **20 Seconds** after they’re opened, before closing. Each of the four surfaces on the monolith can be used to open new portals, and can all be open at the same time.

_The Sub-level of The Rift is referred to as “The Ambry.”_
cards.name: Deep Rifts & The Ambry

cards.desc:
# **Blackouts**

Blackouts are another feature within the game that cause all lights in a map to go out, and causes the map to go dark; restricting all Survivor’s vision to **20 Studs** and granting the **Killer** night vision.
cards.name: Blackouts

cards.desc:
# **Inscriptions**

All maps spawn with **7 Inscriptions** in random locations, often hidden in the environment.

Inscriptions are inactive symbols that have been Inscribed onto the environment, appearing as small, white, rough, chalky circles with foggy, white symbols encased within the circle. Inscriptions always have a piece of chalk next to them. (Reference attached below.)
In this form, they are inactive and have no effect, although can be interacted with by Survivors to undo them. The animation of undoing an Inscription appears as the Survivor wiping their hand over it, smearing it; warping it's appearance. This action requires a base time of **20 seconds** to complete.
Survivors can also reinscribe an Inscription if it has previously been undone, although this requires a base time of **40 seconds** to complete. The animation consist of the Survivor picking up the piece of chalk and inscribing the symbols back into the environment. If a reinscribed Inscription is undone or snuffed a second time, it will completely fade away; burning up into a black fog.
If a Survivor has a [**Glyph Perk**](https://trello.com/c/q7QfisNu/113-glyph-ritual-and-rune-perks "‌"), and a [**Sigil Perk**](https://trello.com/c/YMOV2xc7/114-sigil-perks "‌") is in play; the Survivor can turn the **Sigil** into a **Glyph** directly without having to undo it.

Inscriptions with no active effect are called **Dormant Inscriptions.**
cards.name: Inscriptions

cards.desc:
# **The Depths**

**The Depths** is this game’s version of a “Battle-Pass,” offering rewards on two tracks; the free version and the premium version. The Premium Track is called the **Dark Depths**, and have exclusive cosmetics that will later return for free; although there are **Dark Depth** exclusive skins and cosmetics that will only be available in the **Premium Track**, being the **Dark Depth** themed cosmetics.
cards.name: The Depths (Battle-Pass)

cards.desc:
# **Rarity Meanings/Appearance**

## _Ashen_

Ashen items are of poor origin, having little to no meaning or effect. (_Refractions_) Has weak or small changes to Powers and Items. Ashen Rarity items have a Ash/Smoky gray appearance.

## _Dissonant_

Dissonant items are of poor harmony, still having poor Quality but better, with a weak energy. (_Refractions_) Has slightly stronger changes to Powers and Items. Dissonant Rarity items have a Dusky Violet appearance.

## _Luminous_

Luminous items are the beginning, carrying a gentle glow and of good Quality. (_Refractions_) Has moderate changes to Powers and Items, with a wider range of effects. Luminous Rarity items have a soft gold appearance, although not too bright yet.

## _Radiant_

Radiant items are of powerful origin, carrying a soft glow and energy, and are of great Quality in the way they were crafted. (_Refractions_) Strong changes to Powers and Items, with an even wider range of effects. Radiant Rarity items carry a much softer and lighter Gold appearance, with a Pearl White undertone.

## _Ethereal_

Ethereal items are beautiful, radiating a powerful energy as they glisten with an iridescent light; are of perfect Quality.  (_Refractions_) Large changes to Powers and Items, can change the behavior of them completely. Ethereal Rarity items have a bright glow, and glisten as they reflect light across the entire color spectrum, shining an iridescent glow around them.

## _Veiled_

Veiled items are arcane in nature, emitting a black fog around them; crafted from The Fog’s Essence itself. This Rarity item only comes in the form of **Skins** or the **Veiled Key Item**.
cards.name: Rarity Meanings/Appearance
