cards.desc:
# **Main Goal [Survivor]**

As a Survivor, your main goal is to **stay alive** and **neutralize the Killer**, with your secondary goals being to **Complete all Objectives** and escape with your team. You start with **Two of each Material** in your Backpack/Inventory, accessible by clicking Q while in-game.
✱ _Some maps may have alternative methods for opening the Exit._
✱ _Keybinds can be customized in the settings._

To achieve a Survivor Win, the **Killer must be neutralized** or all objectives must be completed with more than half of the Survivors escaping. Otherwise, it will end in a draw.

Dealing with the Killer can be accomplished through a variety of ways, with **Weapons** and **Tools** spawning on the map alongside **Materials** which can be used to **Craft** more weapons and tools, which include things from **Pistols** to **Rifles** and **Traps.**

As for Objectives, they will **vary between maps**, alongside special **Objective Phases.** The specifics for each Objective differ, and some are only available during certain game stages. Objective Phases advance by completing necessary Objectives displayed in the corner of your screen. Additionally, objectives will scale with player count, with the length of objectives and amount changing accordingly.
cards.name: Main Goal

cards.desc:
# **Survivor Archetypes**

## **Support**

Survivors who excel in helping their teammates, be it by healing, buffs, or providing support in some kind of other way such as traps or buildings.

## **Confrontational**

Survivors who excel in head-on interactions with the Killer, be it fighting them, defensive actions, or generally offensive actions against the Killer.

## **Operator**

Survivors who excel in completing Objectives, often having abilities that interact with Objectives or grant themselves and/or others objective proficiency.

## **Preservation**

Survivors who excel in self-preservation, often having abilities that allow them to escape chase or prolong their own survival in one way or another.

## **Stealth**

Survivors who excel in avoiding the Killer and entities alike, having abilities that grant them stealth in one way or another.

## **Technical**

Survivors with more esoteric abilities that don’t fit in the other categories, often having special powers with special interactions.
cards.name: Survivor Archetypes

cards.desc:
# **Health States & Pools of Blood**

While at **100% Health**, you are considered in the “**Healthy” State.**

- _Below 50% Health: Occasional blood effect, and leave behind **Pools of Blood** every second. These pools will last for **8s** before disappearing._
- _Below 40% Health: Enter the ‘**Injured State,’** clenching your gut with one hand and hunching over._

## **Healing Actions**

**Healing Actions** are any actions taken by **Survivors** to heal themselves or others. These actions include; **Healing (Self or Altruistically), Mending (Fatal Wounds),** or **Recovering (Altruistically).** **Self-Healing** actions progress at **33% Speed.**

- _Healing takes **12s.** (20s Self)_
- _Mending takes a **Fixed 6s.** (10s Self)_
- _Recovering takes **16s.** (27s Self, 95% Cap)_

**Self-Healing** can only be performed with a **Med-Kit** or having a **Perk or Passive** that allows self-healing actions.

**Healing Actions** will prompt **Skill-Checks** occasionally with a **8% Chance** of appearing **every second.**
Missing a **Healing Skill-Check** will trigger a **Loud Noise Notification** at your location.

## **Ticks, Downed State, & Recovery**

At **0 Health,** enter the ‘_Downed’_ State and lose a _'tick.'_

Survivors can be downed a total of **Two Times** before **Dying** if downed a third time. This is shown on the **UI** as ‘_ticks,’_ with Survivors losing a tick each time they are downed, displayed as the tick **burning away.** Upon losing both ticks, a bloody strike will be seen struck through the ticks, symbolizing that it’s your last chance.

While in the **Downed State,** a **240s Timer** will begin ticking down, killing you instantly upon expiry, regardless of your ticks. This timer will continue where left off if Downed again.
Survivors are able to **Fully Recover** to pick themselves up **once** per round.

## **Death**

**Death** is the final state of a Survivor. Dying will remove you from the match. Dying can happen in a multitude of ways, such as **Bleeding Out** when downed, being **Requiemed,** getting downed with **No Ticks,** or through various **Perk Interactions.**

Upon dying, you will immediately **drop all of your items.**
cards.name: Health, Healing Actions, Recovery, Ticks & Death

cards.desc:
# **Survivor Archetypes**

## **Altruistic**

Survivors who excel in helping their teammates, be it by healing, buffs, or generally selfless actions that aid the team directly.

## **Confrontational**

Survivors who excel in head-on interactions with the Killer, be it fighting them, defensive actions, or generally offensive actions against the Killer.

## **Operator**

Survivors who excel in completing Objectives, often having abilities that interact with Objectives or grant themselves and/or others objective proficiency.

## **Preservation**

Survivors who excel in self-preservation, often having abilities that allow them to escape chase or prolong their own survival in one way or another.

## **Stealth**

Survivors who excel in avoiding the Killer and entities alike, having abilities that grant them stealth in one way or another.

## **Technical**

Survivors with more esoteric abilities that don’t fit in the other categories, often having special powers with special interactions.
cards.name: Survivor Archetypes

cards.desc:
# **Locomotion**

The main way Survivors maneuver around the map is a collection of things, such as **Stamina Management, Movement States, Interactables** such as **Vaults,** and more.

## **Movement States**

There are multiple movement states, being **Walking, Sprinting, Exhausted, Fatigue,** and **Crouching.**

- _Walking: Default State. Move at **16 Stud/s.**_
- _Sprinting: Using Stamina to move at **160% Walk Speed.**_
- _Exhausted: Innate **15% Hinder.** Temporary State when out of Stamina. Expires upon recovering Stamina._
- _Fatigue: Innate **25% Hinder.** Temporary State when Running for over **4s.**_
- _Crouching: Move at **40% Walk Speed** but become silent._

## **Stamina**

Stamina is not standardized, instead being a stat for each character which can vary. Stamina is defined by **Max Stamina, Stamina Drain,** and **Stamina Regen.**

There is a **0.6s Delay** before Stamina begins to regenerate.

Characters, upon running out of Stamina, have a **2.5s Delay** before Stamina begins to regenerate once more. If you enter the **Fatigue State,** this delay is increased to **3.5s.**

**Stamina Usage** is **Locked** if Stamina falls below **20%** of **Max Stamina,** and cannot be used again until exceeding that threshold by regenerating Stamina. This does not apply if you continue sprinting when passing the threshold.

## **Interactables**

Interactables are specific **Map Features** that can be interacted with to perform some kind of movement action. This includes things like **Vaults,** taking the form of **Windows, Pallets,** or other miscellaneous objects.
cards.name: Locomotion, Stamina, Interactables

cards.desc:
# **Health States & Pools of Blood**

While at **100% Health**, you are considered in the “**Healthy” State.**

- Below 50% Health: Bleeding effect and leave behind **Pools of Blood** every **1 Second** wherever you go. Pools of Blood persist for **3.5 Seconds.**
- Below 40% Health: Enters the "Injured" State with heavy panting. You will hold your gut while in this state.

## **Healing Actions**

**Healing Actions** are any actions taken by **Survivors** to heal themselves or others. These actions include; **Healing (Self or Altruistically), Mending (**[**Fatal Wounds**](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌")**),** or **Recovering (Altruistically).**

Cancelling a **Healing Action** on a Survivor _(Including Self)_ with [**Haemorrhage**](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌") will cause a **Loud Noise Notification.**

All **Self-Healing** actions are at **33% Speed.** _(Including Mending)_

## **Ticks, Downed State, & Recovery**

Survivors have two _“Ticks,”_ which function as their lives. If a Survivor is downed with no ticks left, they will **die.** Survivors are able to see their current ticks in the bottom corner of their screen, which appear as rough, white lines. Upon losing a tick, the line will burn away and become an ashy grey. Once they lose all ticks and are killed, a red, bloody line will strike through the two ticks. Survivors and Killers can see the current ticks of all the Survivors on their UI.

At 0 Health, enter the "Downed" State and lose one tick and begin a bleed-out timer for 240 seconds. The timer resumes from where it left off if downed again. Passive recovery when staying still is at 50% of normal speed, up to 95% Recovered. Can also be recovered by other Survivors at normal speed.

Survivors are able to recover themselves fully from the downed state **once** per round.

The death icon when bled out _(dying via timer running out while downed)_ is an intact skull with a bloody backdrop.

_Reference:_

![IconHelp\_BleedOutDeath.webp](https://trello.com/1/cards/66972492fe88151363655d0a/attachments/67a6956cbccb5eac510df939/download/IconHelp_BleedOutDeath.webp)

## **Death**

After dying from _Bleeding Out_ or being _Requimed_, the following animation will play if a corpse is left behind: _The Survivor falls to the ground as a black, inky puddle forms underneath them before being grabbed by many tendrils, pulling them down into the puddle._ All of that Survivor’s items are then spewed out from the puddle in the form of a floating, inky black orb before the puddle vanishes.

A soul orb will also be released if the _Requiem_ doesn’t leave a corpse.
cards.name: Health, Healing Actions, Recovery, Ticks & Death

cards.desc:
# **Survival Information**

Surviving involves adapting to avoid, loop, and fight the Killers. Utilise your Heartbeat, Vaults, Wedges, Doors, Tools, Sprint, Running, Crouching, Chase/Looping, and Perks.

When hit by a **Standard Attack** or **Special Attack** (_That deals 20+ Damage in one hit_), you are granted a **2 Second** boost of speed; gaining a **45% Haste** for that duration.

**Chase** is broken once you leave the **Killer’s** **Line-of-Sight** for **5 Seconds**, and is also automatically broken once you are **65 Studs** away from the **Killer**.

## **General Survivability**

`Perks` are one of the core parts of the game, giving special abilities or boosts to Survivors and Killers alike. These are vast in what they do, either giving specific boosts during chase or allowing Survivors to do Objectives faster.
`Chase/Looping` is the main game-play loop, using every single aspect listed above. This is the most important skill to know in the game, as it allows you and your team-mates to survive much longer once mastered. Using the tiles within the game, with the **Vaults**, **Wedges**, and many more; you can have the Killer chasing you for a very long time, buying time for your your team-mates to complete the Objective at hand.
Your `Heartbeat` displays as a beating heart icon on your Character, which grows brighter, larger, and beats faster the closer you are to the **Killer**. This is the Killer's **Terror Radius**.

## **Interactables**

`Vaults` are intractable parts of the map that allow you to move to the other side. Useful for evading the Killer and extending Chase. There are three versions of the **Vault Action**, being **Slow, Medium,** and **Fast** **Vaults**. **Slow** **Vaults** are performed when walking and using a **Vault** while walking or crouching, and takes **1.5 Seconds** to complete. **Medium Vaults** are performed while running and **Vaulting** from any part of the **Vault** that isn’t directly at the **Vault**, and take **0.9 Seconds** to complete. **Fast Vaults** are performed while sprinting from at least **7 Studs** away directly into the **Vault**, and takes **0.5 Seconds** to complete. Performing **Medium** and **Fast** vaults will trigger a **Loud Noise Notification.**
`Wedges` are special parts of maps that allow Survivors to either **Wedges** are special map features that allow Survivors to Wedge through a gap in a wall, which takes **0.7 Seconds** to complete.
`Doors` are also parts of the map that can be interacted with to **open**, **close**, **slam**, and **lock** them.
`Lockers` are too, parts of the map. They can be interacted with to hide inside of them, which will afflict you with **Blindness** for however long you are inside, but will also block all **Aura-reading** from reading your Aura. However, if a Killer finds you inside of one; you will immediately be tethered; being dragged out of the locker.
Lockers take **2 Seconds** to enter normally, but will take only **0.4 Seconds** to enter as a rushed action. _(Rushed Action can be performed by holding shift while entering a Locker.)_
As for Killers, Lockers will take **2.4 Seconds** to **Search** if they are empty, but the **Instant Tether** action for finding a Survivor in a locker will take **2 Seconds.**
`Environmental Hazards` are parts of the map that can be used to your advantage, such as logs that you can bring down to stun the Killer. Most of these are one-time uses, alike Pallets in Dead by Daylight; however some can be used multiple times.
`Tools` are simply things that can be **found** or **crafted** within the Fog to aid in your efforts against the **Killers**. Explained much more in-depth [**here**](https://trello.com/c/z8iXYpWx/156-combat-weapons-tools-and-materials "‌").

## **Locomotion**

`Walking`; While walking, you move at a walkspeed of **18**. Your footsteps are quieter while walking but still audible.
`Sprinting`; Move **+50%** faster (_27 Walkspeed_) while holding `[SHIFT]`
`Stamina`; To _Sprint_, you must consume Stamina. The amount of Stamina a Survivor has and how fast it recharges differs between each of the playable Characters. Stamina drains at **1/s.** Remember that Sprinting and Running is very loud, and your footsteps are very noticeable audibly.
`Running`; Continue to 'Sprint' while out of Stamina. Moves **+25%** faster (_20 Walkspeed_) but leads to Fatigue after 6 seconds. Fatigued movement speed is **+10%** (_19.8 Walkspeed_) with `[SHIFT]`. Remember that Sprinting and Running is very loud, and your footsteps are very noticeable audibly.
`Crouching` allows you to crouch, moving **50% Slower** (_9 Walkspeed_) while crouching and your footsteps are silent.
cards.name: Survival, Locomotion, Chase, & Interactable Info

cards.desc:
You, as a **Survivor,** are not helpless in this game and actually have the ability to **fight back** against the Killer! **Combat** consists of utilizing the tools given to Survivors, i.e the weapons that spawn around the map, just like Materials. If Survivors are able to deal enough damage to the Killer, they are **stunned** for 12 seconds. This time can be used to gain distance from the Killer, and hopefully find safety!

(chances for other items will be listed later)

✱ _When an item can be crafted/repaired with two different Materials, it will prompt a small window below the item to choose which Material to use._
✱ _Survivors will spawn with 2 of every Material, as well as 16 Pistol Ammo, 12 Revolver Ammo, 6 Shotgun Ammo, and 4 Rifle Ammo._

# **Radial Menu and Inventory**

To use certain items, you must open your Radial Menu with Q. In the Radial Menu, you can hover over the item you want to pull out. Items that appear in the Radial Menu include all Med-Kits, Land-Mines, Trip-Mines, Fog Lantern, and Mist Cloak.
As for your **Inventory**, you can open it (which includes the **Crafting Menu**) by pressing `G`. In this menu, you can hover over the items you want to craft and use **LMB** to craft them. You can also use **RMB** on items (except your knife) to drop them.

The **Crafting Menu** operates as a list, and the **Inventory** is organized as follows:

- **Left Side of Your Screen**: All **Guns** listed, including **Ammunition** for them.
- **Right Side of Your Screen**: All of your **Materials** and **Tools**. (Includes Flashbangs.)

### **Example Radial Menu Use:**

1. **Opening the Radial Menu:**
   - Press `Q` to open the Radial Menu.
2. **Selecting an Item:**
   - Use the mouse to hover over the desired item (e.g., Med-Kit, Land-Mine).
   - The item will be highlighted.
3. **Using the Selected Item:**
   - Release `Q` or press LMB to equip the selected item.
   - Use `LMB` to activate or place the item as needed.

# **Crates/Chests**

Crates & Chests will spawn around the map, with anywhere from 5~7 Crates and 3~5 Chests spawning. Crates will guarantee a random Gun or Tool. Chests contain exclusive items. When opened, they emit a pitch black fog from inside; concealing what is beyond. After 120s, they will close; restocking themselves. Respects Item Caps.

## **Crates/Chest Exclusive Items**

Exclusive items with an equal chance of being found between each of them.

- _Fog Lantern_ - A foggy lantern with a ghostly blue flame. On use, exhaust it's light and send out three branching, pathfinding Shadow Trails in random directions. Rekindles it's flame after 80s. Exclusively found in Chests and Nebu's Wares.
- _Mist Cloak_ - A cloak emitting a dark energy, with fog emanating from within it's garb. On use, blends the Survivor’s shadow trail with the surrounding mist, making it almost invisible for 15 seconds. After the effect is over, the cloak will fade away into a black fog, destroying itself. Exclusively found in Chests and Nebu's Wares.
- _Soul Glasses_ - A pair of glasses, bitterly cold to the touch. On use,  dims the user’s vision and **curses** **30%** of their health, rendering it obsolete, but allows the user to see the souls of those in a **70 Stud Range.** Every **5 Seconds** orbs will appear at the targets locations for **2 Seconds.** Enemies are highlighted in **Red** and Survivors are highlighted in **Blue.**

# **Guns**

✱ _Most guns can be found lying around the map, each having a different spawn cap for an entire round. Some can be crafted, while some can only be found. A random weapon is spawned every 60s somewhere on the map, and three will spawn at the start of the game. (Includes those in the 'other weapons' category._

_Rarity Key:_

- _Common/Ashen: 60%_
- _Uncommon/Dissonant: 20%_
- _Rare/Luminous: 13%_
- _Epic/Radiant: 5%_
- _Legendary/Ethereal: 2%_
  —————————
- _Pistol_ - Most common type of gun that spawns. Moderate fire-rate semi-automatic low-recoil pistol with moderate spread and a **12-round magazine.** Takes **2.2 Seconds** to reload. Can be manually crafted using **4x Scrap/6x Rusted Scrap, 4x Gunpowder, 2x Plastic,** and **2x Leather.**
  - Deals **8 Damage** and has **2x CritX.**
  - No spawn cap.
  - Uses Light Ammo.
- _Sub-Machine Gun_ - Uncommon Gun. Very high fire-rate automatic moderate recoil gun with moderate-high spread and a **30-round magazine.** Takes **2.5 Seconds** to reload.
  - Deals **5 Damage** and has a **1.5x CritX.**
  - No spawn cap.
  - Uses Light Ammo.
- _Revolver_ - Uncommon gun. Slow fire-rate single-action high-recoil revolver with a **6-round chamber.** Reloads one bullet at a time, taking **0.4 Seconds** for each bullet. Each shot requires the user to cock the hammer by pressing again, which takes **0.35 Seconds** to complete before another shot can be fired.
  - Deals **25 Damage** and has **3x CritX.**
  - Spawn cap of **3.**
  - Uses LR Ammo.
- _Pump Shotgun_ - Uncommon gun. Slow fire-rate pump-action high-recoil shotgun with a moderate spread and a **5-round magazine**. Each shot requires the user to pump the shotgun, which takes **0.5 Seconds.** Reloads one shell at a time with a **0.5 Second Interval** between each shell and pumping it after reloading. Reloading can be cancelled early.
  - Shoots **8 Pellets** per shot, with each pellet dealing **12 Damage** with a **1.2x CritX.** Deals **+25% Damage** at close range.
  - Spawn cap of **5.**
  - Uses Shells.
- _Hunting Rifle_ - Rare gun. Slow fire-rate bolt-action high-recoil rifle with little to no spread and a **5-round magazine.** Takes **2 Seconds** to reload. Has a focusing time, requiring **0.4 Seconds** to focus upon scoping in, going from little spread to no spread upon focusing. Hip-firing has additional spread. Each shot requires the user to pull back the bolt to chamber the next shot, which takes **0.7 Seconds.**
  - Deals **50 Damage** and has a **5x CritX.**
  - Spawn cap of **2.**
  - Uses HC Ammo.
- _Assault Rifle_ - Rare gun. High fire-rate automatic assault rifle with low-moderate spread and a **30-round magazine.** Takes **2.7 Seconds** to reload.
  - Deals **12 Damage** and has a **1.5x CritX.**
  - Spawn cap of **4.**
  - Uses Intermediate Ammo.
- _Tactical Shotgun_ - Epic gun. Moderate fire-rate semi-automatic moderate-recoil shotgun with low-moderate spread and a **8-round magazine.** Takes **2.7 Seconds** to reload.
  - Shoots **8 Pellets** per shot, with each pellet dealing **10 Damage** with a **1.5x CritX.**
  - Spawn cap of **1.**
  - Uses Shells.
- _Sniper Rifle_ - Epic gun. Slow fire-rate bolt-action high-recoil rifle with little to no spread and a **3-round magazine.** Takes **2.5 Seconds** to reload. Has a sniper scope with focusing time, requiring **0.5 Seconds** to focus upon scoping in. Hip-firing has additional spread. Each shot requires the user to pull back the bolt to chamber the next shot, which takes **0.7 Seconds.**
  - Deals **100 Damage** and has a **5x CritX.**
  - Spawn cap of **1.**
  - Uses HC Ammo.
- _Deagle_ - Legendary gun. Slow fire-rate semi-automatic high-recoil high-powered pistol with little to no spread and a **7-round magazine.** Takes **2.8 Seconds** to reload.
  - Deals **150 Damage** and has a **3x CritX.**
  - Spawn cap of **1.**
  - Uses HC Ammo.

# **Melee Weapons**

Broken weapons aren’t destroyed, but persist in the inventory as an unusable broken item and can be repaired for double the material cost.

- _Knife_ - The weapon all Survivors spawn with. A standard tactical knife. Has a quick swing speed with little to no cooldown. Has **10 Durability.** Can add **+10 Durability** using **1x Metal Scrap.**
  - Deals **50 Damage.**
- _Bat_ - Uncommon weapon. A wooden bat. Has a slow swing speed with **0.8 Seconds** of wind up, **Stunning** for **1 Second** on-hit. Has **5 Durability.** Can add **+5 Durability** using **1x Tape.**
  - Deals **30 Damage.**
- _Ballistic Knife_ - Epic weapon. A ballistic knife with a cartridge inside the handle. Has quick swing speed. Has **15 Durability.** Can add **+15 Durability** using **1x Metal Scrap & 1x Powder.**
  - Deals **35 Damage** and inflicts **Poison II** for **+2 Seconds.** Consecutive hits are additive to the duration.
- _Sword_ - Epic weapon. A claymore-style iron sword. Has slow, wide swing speed and pierces, hitting through all enemies ahead. Has **20 Durability.** Can add **+10 Durability** with **2x Metal Scrap.**
  - Deals **80 Damage.**

# **Tools**

✱ _Charges are reduced by 1 every second._
✱ _Most Tools can be found lying around the maps, with each having a different chance to spawn. A random Tool is spawned every 30s somewhere on the map, and six will spawn at the start of the game in random areas._
✱ _Survivors can only carry up to 2 of each item, with exceptions being; Flashlights, Backpacks, and Toolboxes._
✱ _For clarification on Charges; they are additive, meaning a Rusted Toolbox can only shave off 35 Charges of a Generator still, just at a 35% faster rate. This logic is the same for Med-Kits, so extra charges won't be used up if you heal yourself instead of someone else at a faster speed; it's just slower._

_Rarity Key:_

- _Common/Ashen: 40%_
- _Uncommon/Dissonant: 30%_
- _Rare/Luminous: 20%_
- _Epic/Radiant: 8%_
- _Legendary/Ethereal: 2%_
  —————————
- a

# **Special Tools**

✱ _Special Tools are Active Tools that can be equipped and used with the Radial Menu._

# **Ammunition**

✱ _Ammunition packs can also be found around the maps. A pack of random Ammunition will spawn every 40s at a random location, having one magazine worth of whichever Gun it is for, except Rifle Ammo spawning at 3x a pack._

- _12x Low-Caliber Ammo_ - Can be crafted with 3x Rusted Scrap and 2x Gunpowder.
- _6x LR Ammo_ - Can be crafted with 1x Scrap, 3x Rusted Scrap, and 2x Gunpowder.
- _5x Shells_ - Can be crafted with 3x Scrap, 3x Gunpowder, and 2x Plastic.
- _20x Intermediate Ammo_ - Can be crated with 2x Scrap, 1x Metal Scrap, 2x Gunpowder, and 1x Plastic.
- _3x HC Ammo_ - Can be crafted with 3x Scrap, 1x Metal Scrap, 3x Gunpowder, and 2x Plastic.

# **Materials**

✱ _Materials are found scattered across maps. A Material will respawn every 2s in a random location, and every 60s a large batch of 30x Materials will respawn. Locations that Materials spawn at vary across maps, and can be; on the ground, on shelves, in drawers or storage containers, or scattered around buildings. Each type has different spawn rates._

- _Rarity Key:_
  - _Common/Ashen: 40% (55%)_
  - _Uncommon/Dissonant: 32% (25%)_
  - _Rare/Luminous: 16% (15%)_
  - _Epic/Radiant: 8% [N/A]_
  - _Legendary/Ethereal: 4% [N/A]_

✱\_Survivors can carry a base amount of up to 8 of each Material.\_

#### _Common:_

- _Scrap_ - Common material [33%]
- _Rusted Scrap_ - Common material [33%]
- _Herb_ - Common material [33%]

#### _Uncommon:_

- _Plastic_ - Uncommon material [30%]
- _Tape_ - Uncommon material [30%]
- _Gunpowder_ - Uncommon material [22%]
- _Battery_ - Uncommon material [18%]

#### _Rare:_

- _Metal Scrap_ - Rare material [40%]
- _Leather_ - Rare material [32%]
- _Powder_ - Rare material [28%]

#### _Epic:_

- _N/A_

#### _Legendary:_

- _N/A_
cards.name: Combat, Weapons, Tools, and Materials

cards.desc:
✱ _Interactions: Add Charges at a default rate of 1 Charge per second. This is equivalent to an Action speed of 100%._
✱ _Interactions are only available during the corresponding Objective Phase._

As mentioned in the Main Goal card, there are many Objectives to be found in the game. This card will explain each of them in-depth as they are made.
Objectives can only be performed in their specific Objective Phase. The Objectives that appear in each phase are picked from randomly from a pool of objectives, which are specific for each map.

# **Primary Objective: KILL**

Shoot the killer.

# **Rift Anchors [Secondary Objective]**

**Rift Anchors** are mysterious machinations used to drive back the encroaching threat of the **Deep Rift**, assuring it does not break through it’s layer of reality and plunge the **Rift** and reality as we know it into unspeakable madness.

**6 Rift Anchors** will appear in random allotted spots on the map at the beginning of the round, and will remain offline until the round timer reaches **3:00**.

Rift Anchors are the main objective outside of **Killer Neutralization**, allowing Survivors to gain more time in the match to push back against the Killers. Rift Anchors will require Survivors to complete a series of mini-games/puzzles to power them, in which they will stay online for **90 Seconds** before disabling again. Each time a Rift Anchor is powered, it will add **1:30 Minutes** to the timer.

_Rift Anchor Puzzles:_

- _#1. Input Code [Small Math Problem for a Four Digit Code]_
- _#2a. Terminal Hacking 1 [3x Snake Mini-Game, Avoid Walls To Reach Exit Box]_
- _#2b. Terminal Hacking 2 [3x Type Codestring, fill-in text]_
- _#3. Enable Modules [Turn on the 4~7 Correct Options out of 10, provided a list of names (Example: RA.B7102)]_
- _#4. Finalize Inputs [10 Second Loading Bar]_

If any of these puzzles are failed or interrupted, the Rift Anchor will become locked out for **10 Seconds.**

# **Map Objectives [Tertiary Objectives]**

Map objectives are separate objectives or objective types that will almost always be present in any given match, being tasks dependent on the current map. These are often in the form of a generator-style objective that takes around **30-60 Charges** to complete with Skill-Checks periodically appearing, length and amount scaling with player count.

## **Exits**

Exits are always going to be present as the final objective after other objectives are completed, with each map having a unique exit mechanic. Once an exit has been opened, Survivors can simply leave through the exit.
cards.name: Objectives Information

cards.desc:
# **Skill Checks**

Skill Checks are **Quick Time Events (QTE)** that will appear for **Survivors**. Skill Checks appear on screen as a circle in the middle of the screen with a rotating pointer, which Survivors will have to press either **[SPACE] or LMB** (Tap on Mobile, X/Square on Console) to hit the **Skill Check**.
On the circle, there is a **Success Zone** which takes up 24% of the Circle. The **Success Zone** has two parts; of which 16% of the **Success Zone** is transparent and when hit will be a "**Good Skill Check**", while the other 6% of it is a solid white and when hit will be a "**Great Skill Check**."
**Skill Checks** have an **8% Chance** to appear **once every second.**

![SkillChecks.webp](https://trello.com/1/cards/669811c80ed7381b621632c4/attachments/67fd7663020c6b5aaac7cfbb/download/SkillChecks.webp)

## **Good Skill Check**

Succeed a **Skill Check** in the transparent section of the **Success Zone**:

- Grants **50 Points** in the **Objective Category**

## **Great Skill Check**

Succeed a **Skill Check** in the solid white section of the **Success Zone**:

- Grants **300 Points** in the **Objective Category** and a 1.5% **Progression** Boost for Repair actions.
- Grants **150 Points** in the **Altruism Category** and a 3% **Progression** Boost for Healing actions.

## **Failed Skill Check**

Failing to Succeed a **Skill Check** in any form within the game:

- Pause the interaction for 3 Seconds and incur a **10%** Progression penalty for all actions, as well as giving the **Killer** a **Loud Noise Notification**.
cards.name: Skill Checks

cards.desc: Communication is key in team play. Use the Communications tool to provide quick information to your teammates. Accessible by a small menu on the right side of your screen.
cards.name: Communication

cards.desc:
These Perks are Unique Perks that differ from the usual when it comes to Perks, and require extra explanations for how they function.

# **Glyph Perks**

**Glyph** Perks function identically to [**Sigil**](https://trello.com/c/YMOV2xc7/114-sigil-perks "‌") **Perks**, in the aspect that they are activated from [**Inscriptions**](https://trello.com/c/RekOItRk/120-inscriptions "‌"). To activate a **Glyph**, a **Survivor** must **Inscribe** one of the **Inscriptions**. A Survivor can also **Bless** an active **Sigil** to turn it into a **Glyph**, as well. **Killers** can snuff out **Glyphs** by interacting with them, with the action taking **1 Second** to complete.

**Glyphs** have a constant **droning sound** that plays within a **60 Stud Radius**, growing louder the closer you are to it.

# **Ritual Perks**

**Ritual** Perks are special Perks that can only be performed in specific areas that are designated depending on the map. These Perks are extremely powerful and always have a significant effect, but also require a significant amount of time to perform.
**Ritual** Perks grant the Survivor using them the ability to perform a **Ritual**, which is performed by kneeling down and sitting on your knees as energy begins to fill the air around you. The closer the **Ritual** is to it's completion, the more energy fills the air around it, and the brighter the **Ritual Circle** becomes, and when a **Ritual** is finished; a column of winding energy is blasted into the sky and a loud "_woosh_" sound rips through the air.

# **Rune Perks**

**Rune** Perks are special Perks that give a strong ability to the Survivor, but is limited in use; having a limited number of "**Charges**" before the **Rune** breaks. **Runes** are visible accessories that are connected to a **necklace**, having multiple **Runes** on the necklace depending on how many **Rune** Perks are equipped. Different **Rune** Perks have different effects, and also different activation requirements to trigger their effects. Some may require you to be in chase, hit with a Standard Attack, or may be an active ability.
cards.name: Glyph, Ritual, and Rune Perks

cards.desc:
On the **Survivor** side, there are **four** distinct scores that can be increased via **in-game actions**. Each of these increase with different actions, and depending on the Survivor's **performance** up until the end of the game, they will receive **Fog Essence** with a **1:1 Ratio** for the amount of **Points** gained in each category.

**XP** is rewarded at a rate of **5:1 Fog Essence** to **XP** at the end of a round.

The four scores are as follows;

- _Objectives_
  The **Objectives Score** can be increased by various interactions with **Objectives**, such as **working on Objectives**, **completing Objectives**, **working** on **Objectives** **with other Survivors**, **completing Skill Checks**, or **opening the Veiled Door** after it has been **closed**. There are more Score Events to trigger and gain Score in Objectives, and I will list them individually in this card at a later date.
- _Survival_
  The **Survival Score** can be increased by various interactions regarding **Surviving** with in the Fog, such as; **Ending chases**, time spent not in the **Injured State, Dying State** or in a **Foghold**, **healing self**, **succeeding Self-Heal Skill Checks**, and **Escaping**. There are more Score Events to trigger and gain Score in Survival, and I will list them individually in this card at a later date.
- _Altruism_
  The **Altruism Score** can be increased by various interactions regarding **helping other Survivors**, such as; **Healing other Survivors**, **Recovering other Survivors**, **diverting chase** to **you** while **another Survivor** is in **chase** (will only trigger if chase ends for the other Survivor. Extra points if the Survivor is in the Injured State), and taking **Protection Hits**. There are more Score Events to trigger and gain Score in Altruism, and I will list them individually in this card at a later date.
- _Boldness_
  The **Boldness Score** can be increased by various interactions with the Killer, such as; **chase time**, **stunning** the **Killer**, **damaging** the **Killer**, **healing** while **in chase**, **saving** while in **chase**, **undoing Sigils**, being within the Killer's **terror radius**, **escaping Chase**, and **dodging Standard Attacks**. There are more Score Events to trigger and gain Score in Boldness, and I will list them individually in this card at a later date.

## **Miscellaneous Scores**

There also exists Misc. Scores, being those that do not fit within the main 4 Scores or are part of an on-going event. These will be listed at some point.
cards.name: Rewards & Performance

cards.desc:
###### _Ditto of the other_ [_**Aura & Instinct Card**_](https://trello.com/c/Wm8eSxbj/211-aura-instinct "‌")

# **Aura**

**Aura Reading** is the main detection function of the game, with **“Aura”** revealing the character effected by highlighting their character model through the walls in red. **Aura Detection** can be denied by hiding inside of a **Locker** as **Survivor**, and **Status Effects** like **Blindness** will disable **Aura Reading** entirely.

Additionally, the Auras of Generators are always visible to the **Killer** in **Red.** Blocked Generators are shown in **White**, and Generators with active Killer effects on them are shown in **Yellow.**

# **Instinct**

Instinct is a special **Killer-Exclusive** mechanic that serves as another way of detection besides **Aura Reading**, which simply highlights the **Aura** of the character effected. **Instinct** will instead display a pulsating red spiderweb (?) effect with a beating heart sound to notify you of it’s presence. Unlike **Aura Reading**, **Instinct** cannot be denied by any means and serves as an uncounterable reveal effect.
cards.name: Aura & Instinct (Ditto)

cards.desc:
# **Killer Archetypes**

## **Control**

Killers who excel in controlling the match, such as slowdown, gen-control, map control, and zoning; among other things. These _aren’t_ mutually inclusive.

## **Chase**

Killers who excel during chases with Survivors, having abilities that compliment the hunt.

## **Hexer**

Killers who excel at applying status effects onto Survivors, being one of their main abilities.

## **Stealth**

Killers who excel at stealth, being able to hide themselves as they hunt down Survivors.

## **Summoner**

Killers who revolve around their ability to summon entities, overwhelming the map or Survivors with these entities.

## **Technical**

Killers who don’t particularly fit into the other categories, having very special powers or unique and varied abilities that give them a special designation.
cards.name: Killer Archetypes
Chase & Survivor Interactions (Stamina, Requiems, Shadows of the Fog, Bloodlust)

cards.desc:
# **Health & Stunned**
As a Killer, you also have Health. Each Killer has a different Base HP, which increases based on the number of Survivors. Your Health will rarely be at its Base HP (100%), as this is based on a single Survivor. For each additional Survivor, your Base HP increases by 12.5%. This means that with the maximum number of Survivors, your Base HP will be 200% (or +100%).

When your HP is reduced to 0, you will enter the **Stunned** State, causing you to fall to the ground. Each Killer may have a unique Stun Animation. During this state, the base duration of the stun is 10 seconds. You cannot take any actions except for looking around.

## **End-Game**
During the End-Game (when Exit Switches are powered), your Base HP is doubled. However, your HP is recalculated based on the number of remaining Survivors. For example, if the game started with the maximum Survivor Count (9) and your Base HP was 2,250, you would have 4,500 HP (200%). If, during End-Game, only 4 Survivors remain, your HP would be 6,750 instead of the full 9,000 HP.
cards.name: Health

cards.desc:
# **Requiem**

**Requiem** is a special interaction within the game that allows **Killers** to instantly **Kill** **Survivors** without **Banishing** them. This can be attained by using [**Requiem Catalysts**](https://trello.com/c/CgQpmzPf/282-catalysts-refractions "‌"), **Specific** [**Refractions**](https://trello.com/c/CgQpmzPf/282-catalysts-refractions "‌"), or even **Killer Powers**.

**Every single Killer** has a **Requiem**, with different special **Animations** for it specifically. **Requiems** each have their own length.
cards.name: Requiems

cards.desc:
On the **Killer** side, there are **four** distinct scores that can be increased via **in-game actions**. Each of these increase with different actions, and depending on the Killer's **performance** up until the end of the game, they will receive **Fog Essence** with a **1:1 Ratio** for the amount of **Points** gained in each category.

The four scores are as follows;

- _Domination_
  The **Domination Score** can be increased through actions that showcase your strength as a Killer. This includes achieving **Downs** on Survivors, delivering **Hits**, **destroying Doors**, and **damaging Generators**. More actions can increase this score, and specifics will be detailed later.
- _Malice_
  The **Malice Score** is boosted by effectively using your Killer powers and ensuring no Survivors escape. This includes successfully activating your abilities, downing Survivors with your abilities, and grants a massive boost to this score if no one escapes. More actions can increase this score, and specifics will be detailed later.
- _Pursuit_
  The **Pursuit Score** can be earned by engaging in chases and locating Survivors. This includes the duration of chases, finding Survivors, and pulling them out of Lockers. More actions can increase this score, and specifics will be detailed later.
- _Execution_
  The **Execution Score** is focused on your ability to capture Survivors and secure kills. This includes downing Survivors, achieving eliminations, or performing Requiems. More actions can increase this score, and specifics will be detailed later.
cards.name: Rewards & Performance

cards.desc:
As a Killer, you can interact with certain Objectives; namely **Generators** and the **Veiled Door**.

# **Generators**
By interacting with a Generator, you can **Damage** it; immediately taking off 8 Charges and leaving it to regress at a rate of -0.5c/s. **Damaged** Generators will spew sparks.

# **Veiled Door**
By interacting with a **Veiled Door**, you slam it shut; sealing it off. Survivors **cannot escape** through a **Closed Veiled Door**, although they can open it with a **Veiled Key.** By closing the **Veiled Door**, all **Objective Phases** are automatically **skipped** and the **Exit Switches** become **powered**.
cards.name: Objective Interactions

cards.desc:
# **Sigil Perks**
**Sigil** Perks are Perks which abilities originate from **[Inscriptions](https://trello.com/c/RekOItRk/120-inscriptions)**, lighting them up and turning them into a **Sigil** once the Perk's activation requirements have been met. These are uniquely powerful Perks, but have a huge downside of being able to be disabled entirely by **Survivors** in the form of undoing the **Inscription**, disabling it for the rest of the match.
cards.name: Sigil Perks

cards.desc:
# **Shadows of The Fog**
## *"These shadows are the lingering imprints of The Fog’s influence, manifesting as those of us move through this accursed place. They represent the ever-present reach of The Fog, watching our every move.."*
*- Unknown Writer*

Or for short, 'Shadows,' or 'Shadow Trail,' is a feature of the game that allows Killers to track Survivors. When Survivors sprint, they leave behind Shadows of The Fog on the environment around them. These shadows are faint, misty imprints that linger in the environment, giving Killers a way to track their movements. Newer imprints (3~4 seconds old) are also accompanied by a misty silhouette of the Survivor running.
The Shadows appear as wispy, misty areas in the shape of footprints, hand-prints, or wisps of fog that slightly blend with the ambient mist of the environment. They are less noticeable than the Scratch Marks from Dead by Daylight or Running Trails from Trepidation but become clearer when viewed closely by Killers.
Shadows of The Fog linger for a short period (around ~8 seconds), slowly dissipating into the environment. They start off light and prominent but gradually fade, making it crucial for Killers to act quickly to follow the trail.
cards.name: Shadows of The Fog

cards.desc:
# **Bloodlust**

**Bloodlust** is a **Killer-Exclusive Tiered Status Effect** that gives you a **Walk-Speed Bonus** depending on the **Duration** of **Individual Chases**.

- _Tier I Bloodlust: After 15 Seconds in Chase, +1 Walk-Speed_
- _Tier II Bloodlust: After 30 Seconds in Chase, +2 Walk-Speed_
- _Tier III Bloodlust: After 45 Seconds in Chase, +4 Walk-Speed_

You can lose **Bloodlust** if one of the following events happen; Use a **Standard Attack** (_Misssed or Successful_), Use an **Ability**, Get **Stunned**, or breaks a **Door**.
cards.name: Bloodlust

cards.desc:
# **Fixation**

**Fixation** is a mechanic that works similarly to **Obsession** in **Dead By Daylight**. It has no function by itself, and it purely determined by **Perks, Passives, Powers,** **Refractions,** and **Catalysts**.

A **Fixation** is determined at random with an equal chance for each Survivor at the beginning of every match, regardless of if there is something that effects **Fixations** present. You will get a **Survival Score** bonus if you **Escape** as the **Fixation**.

If there are Perks that manipulate the chance of becoming the Fixation, it will take from the chances of other Survivors.

_For Example:_

1. 8 Survivors, each having a 12.5% Chance to become the initial/first Fixation upon the match starting.
2. 1 Survivor brings a Perk that increases their chance of being the initial Fixation by +100%.
3. That Survivor now has a ~25% Chance to become the initial Fixation.
4. Each of the other 7 Survivors now have an ~11% Chance to become the Fixation.

This means that it will not guarantee that Survivors become the initial Fixation or prevent them from becoming the initial Fixation, because the chance modifiers are applied to the _base_ chance of becoming the initial Fixation.

![Sprite-0002.jpeg](https://trello.com/1/cards/67008a03052a75c050d4106b/attachments/677c7e202c3ce334026bb64a/download/Sprite-0002.jpeg)

###### _Normal Survivor Icon Border & Fixated Survivor Icon Border_
cards.name: Fixation

cards.desc:
# **Terror Radius**

**Terror Radii** are one of the core mechanics in **D:R**, being the radius around the **Killer** that causes the **Heartbeat** within it’s range, and increasing with proximity to the **Killer**, growing the closer and closer they come.

For math reasons and easier understanding, the **Killer**’s **Terror Radius** is _usually_ **300%** of **Walk-Speed**.
cards.name: Terror Radius

cards.desc:
# **Aura**

**Aura Reading** is the main detection function of the game, with **“Aura”** revealing the character effected by highlighting their character model through the walls in red. **Aura Detection** can be denied by hiding inside of a **Locker** as **Survivor**, and **Status Effects** like **Blindness** will disable **Aura Reading** entirely.

Additionally, the Auras of Generators are always visible to the **Killer** in **Red.** Blocked Generators are shown in **White**, and Generators with active Killer effects on them are shown in **Yellow.**

_Basic Killer Aura-reading Colours:_

- **Active Generators: Red**
- **Blocked Generators: White**
- **Effected Generators: Yellow**
- **Inscriptions: Red**
- **Blocked Inscriptions: White**
- **Sigils: Red**
- **Glyphs: Blue**
- **Entities: Blue**
- **Survivors: Red**

_Basic Survivor Aura-reading Colours:_

- **Active Generators: Red**
- **Blocked Generators: White**
- **Effected Generators: Yellow**
- **Inscriptions: Red**
- **Blocked Inscriptions: White**
- **Sigils: Red**
- **Glyphs: Blue**
- **Entities: Red**
- **Killers: Red**
- **Survivors: Blue**

# **Instinct**

Instinct is a special **Killer-Exclusive** mechanic that serves as another way of detection besides **Aura Reading**, which simply highlights the **Aura** of the character effected. **Instinct** will instead display a pulsating red spider-web (?) effect with a beating heart sound to notify you of it’s presence. Unlike **Aura Reading**, **Instinct** cannot be denied by any means and serves as an uncounterable reveal effect.
cards.name: Aura & Instinct

cards.desc:
###### Uses heavy inspiration from The Wraith’s Add-ons from Dead by Daylight.

# **The Shade’s Refractions**

## _Ashen Rarity_

- _Faux Veil - “A crude recreation of the Cloak.“_
  - Gain **Concealed** for **4 Seconds** after uncloaking.
  - Grants **+25%** bonus Fog Essence for _Surprise Attack_ Score Events.
- _Replicant - “A contained replicant of one of the drones sent out by the Shade.“_
  - Increases the **Walkspeed** of _The Countless_ clones by **1.5.**
  - Decreases the **Bind Time** of \_The Countless \_by **2 Seconds.**
  - Decreases _The Countless_ clone count by **1.**
  - Grants **+150%** bonus Fog Essence for _Dark Bind_ and _Helpless Gash_ Score Events.
- _“Mischief” Ash - “A symbol representing mischief and trickery, drawn in ash onto the waist of the Cloak.”_
  - No longer gain **Concealed** when _Cloaked_.
  - Grants **+100%** bonus Fog Essence for _Stalking, Ambush Strikes,_ and _Surprise Attack_ Score Events.
- _“The Apple” Ash - “A symbol representing temptation, crudely drawn in ash onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - When _Cloaked,_ [Pools of Blood](https://trello.com/c/F1CtV4MT/77-health-recovery-tethering-foghold-stages "‌") appear to you in bright red.
  - When _Cloaked,_ the **Auras** of any Survivors below **100% Health** are revealed for **3 Seconds** longer any time they are revealed.
  - When _Cloaked,_ walking over Pools of Blood will grant you a short **0.4 Second 50% Haste.**

## _Dissonant Rarity_

- _Perthro - “A rune of mystery; inscribed directly onto the waist of The Shade’s Cloak.“_
  - Survivors are unable to discern the distance and direction of the _Cloak_. _(Both the Screech and Rising Tone play for all Survivors with an echo, not giving information on the direction or distance of the uncloaking SFX.)_

- _“The Shadows” Ink - “A symbol representing the shadows of the Veil, painted in ink onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Increase the maximum distance of _Shaded Step_ by **16 Studs.**

- _“Dancer” Ink - “A symbol representing a serene darkness, painted in ink onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Increases the action speed of all damage, destroy, vaulting, and wedging actions by **40%** while cloaked.
  - Increase **Ambush Strike** penalty cooldown by **0.5 Seconds.**
- _“The Ripple” Ink - “A symbol representing ripples sent through the Veil, wading through the endless darkness; painted in ink onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Increases your movement speed while _Cloaked_ by **5%.**
- _“The Hunt” Ink - “A symbol representing the unending hunt, painted with ink onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - **Reduces** the Uncloaking time by **-6%**.

## _Luminous Rarity_

- _“The Shadows” Bone - “A symbol representing a serene darkness, drawn with dust of bone onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Increase the maximum distance of _Shaded Step_ by **24 Studs.**
- _“Dancer” Bone - “A symbol representing a serene darkness, drawn with dust of bone onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Increases the action speed of all damage, destroy, vaulting, and wedging actions by **60%** while cloaked.
  - Increase **Ambush Strike** penalty cooldown by **0.5 Seconds.**
- _“The Ripple” Bone - “A symbol representing ripples sent through the Veil, wading through the endless darkness; drawn with dust of bone onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Increases your movement speed while _Cloaked_ by **8%.**
- _“The Hunt” Bone - “A symbol representing the unending hunt, drawn with the dust of bone onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Reduces the Uncloaking time by **-10%**.
- _“Enlightening Darkness” Bone - “A symbol representing the hubris of seeking knowledge, drawn with dust of bone onto the waist of the Cloak.“_
  - Survivors hit with an _Ambush Strike_ or _Surprise Attack_ are afflicted with **Blindness** for **60 Seconds** and **Darkness** for **25 Seconds.**

## _Radiant Rarity_

- _Black Spine - “The spine of The Shade’s first victim; blackened by it’s time spent within the Veil. Same as the poor sod’s other preserved bones; in pristine condition and cleaned of all viscera.“_
  - Grants _The Countless_ clones a blackened spine down their backs.
  - Increases the **Walkspeed** of _The Countless_ clones by **2.**
  - Decreases _The Countless_ clone count by **2.**
- _Dark Coating - “A viscous substance, used to coat the claws of the Shade; allowing for deeper cuts into flesh.“_
  - Increase **Standard Attack Damage** to **40.**
  - Reduce **Walkspeed** while _Cloaked_ to **23.**
- _“The Ripple” Blood - “A symbol representing ripples sent through the Veil, wading through the endless darkness; stamped with blood onto the collar of the Cloak.“_
  - Increases your movement speed while _Cloaked_ by **11%.**
- _“The Hunt” Blood - “A symbol representing the unending hunt, stamped with blood onto the collar of the Cloak.“_
  - Reduces the Uncloaking time by **-14%**.

## _Ethereal Rarity_

- _Black Skull - “A skull inscribed with various marks; ripped directly from The Shade’s first victim. It emits an ominous fog from it’s sockets and has blackened over the time it’s spent within the Veil. It’s now kept as a keepsake by The Shade, displaying a disturbing level of intelligence and depravity.“_
  - Upon using _Unto Sin,_ the silhouette of a black skull appears around your head composed of a black fog.
  - Halves the Blackout Duration from _Otherworldly Shroud_ and the distance of _Shaded Step._
  - Increase **Walkspeed** to **22.5.**
  - Removes the **Concealed** status during _Unto Sin._
  - Doubles your **Terror Radius** during _Unto Sin._
  - All Survivors within your Terror Radius during _Unto Sin_ gain **Exhausted.**
  - Extend the duration of _Unto Sin_ by **25 Seconds.**
  - Doubles the damage requirement for _Unto Sin._
  - Removes the **Darkness** status infliction during _Unto Sin._
  - _Unto Sin_ attacks now inflict **Bleeding II.**
- _“The Collective“ Soul - “An iridescent, runic, symbol of a black apple inscribed onto the back of the cloak; containing the forsaken souls and blood of those who were unfortunate enough to be lost wondering the Veil.“_
  - Reveals the Auras of all **Injured Survivors** while _Cloaked._
  - Reveals the Repair progression of **Generators** via the intensity of their **Aura** when _Cloaked. (Grows redder as Repair progression nears completion.)_
  - Lose the ability to perform **Ambush Strikes.**
- _The Veil - “An ominous cloak, seemingly with the ability to veil anything it covers. Although, anything it veils has never been recovered, at least not whole. As if it, or something, doesn’t want to be tampered with.“_
  - Silences _Cloak_ entirely, suppressing Shade’s screech and the rising tone emanating from it’s cloak.
cards.name: Shade

cards.desc:
# **Hunter’s Refractions**

## _Ashen Rarity_

- _Padded Jaws - “Padding over the teeth of the beartrap, designed for capturing the target uninjured.”_
  - Start with **1 Extra Beartrap.** Beartraps no longer deal damage or inflict **Deep Wounds**, instead inflicting **10% Hinder** for **8 Seconds** after the Survivor is freed but will reward **+100% Fog Essence** for trapping Survivors.
- _Safety Arrows - “Arrows designed for training, with padded tips.“_
  - Lower _‘Hunter’s Expertise'_ Cooldown by **15 Seconds**, but no longer deals damage, pierces, or inflicts **Bleed.** Successful arrow hits will reward **+250% Fog Essence.** _(Lace will still cause Poison on-hit.)_
- _Steel Wire - “A sturdy steel wire designed more for durability than damage.“_
  - Tripwires no longer inflict **Bleed**, but will last for two triggers before breaking. Grants **+100% Fog Essence** for successful tripwire activation.
- _Old Leather Gloves - “Dusty old trap-setting gloves.“_
  - Increases **Setting Speed** by **15%.**

## _Dissonant Rarity_

- _Old Satchel - “A dusty satchel with an extra pocket.“_
  - Start with **2 Extra Beartrap** and **+1 Tripwire Limit.**
- _Motion Detector - “An additional attachment module that sends an alert when detecting movement.“_
  - Increases the **Aura Reveal** duration of **Game Cameras** by **+3 Seconds.**
- _Protective Tape - “High-visibility tape to prevents accidental injury.“_
  - Can no longer be caught in your own beartraps.
- _4-Coil Spring Kit & Additional Braiding - “A more complex system, packing more power behind it’s springs.“_
  - Disarm traps now takes **33% Longer.**
- _Tar Bottle - “A bottle filled with a dark tar, designed to conceal and darken anything it’s placed on.“_
  - Darkens the appearance of traps.
- _Blackened Tarp - “A dark, black tarp designed to keep things hidden within the dead of night.“_
  - Placing, Resetting, Setting, Picking Up, Opening, Revealing Survivors, and Exiting Traps or Cameras now grants **Concealed** for **12 Seconds.**

## _Luminous Rarity_

- _Satchel - “A new, roomy satchel used for carrying items.“_
  - Start with **3 Extra Beartraps** and **+2 Tripwire Limit.**
- _Lubricant - “A lubricant applied onto traps to make it more difficult for prey to escape as easily.“_
  - Increases the amount of time it takes to perform **Escape Attempts** and **Rescue Actions** by **40%.**
- _Tool Kit - “A tool kit, allowing for more complex set-up of traps.“_
  - Increases **Setting Speed** by **35%** and Increases the amount of time it takes to perform **Escape Attempts** and **Rescue Actions** by **25%.**
- _Motion Detector MKII - “A direct upgrade from the previous model, allowing for more advanced tracking of targets.“_
  - Increases the **Aura Reveal** duration of **Game Cameras** by **+6 Seconds.**
- _Serrated Teeth - “The serrated jaws of beartraps tear through flesh, severing blood vessels and maiming flesh.“_
  - Survivors caught in traps are now afflicted with **Mangled** until healed.
- _Lengthened Teeth - “The longer teeth of the beartraps dig deep into the flesh, causing much deeper wounds.“_
  - Survivors caught in traps are now afflicted with **Fatal Wounds.**
- _Electrolyte Tablets - “Keeping your energy maintained during long hunts is of utmost importance.“_
  - Increases the **Haste** gained during **Trap Interactions** by an additional **6%.**
- _Thick Tar - “A darker, thicker tar.“_
  - Further darkens the appearance of traps.
- _Hip Quiver - “A quiver worn on the hip, allowing for the carrying of extra arrows.“_
  - Lowers the cooldown of **Hunter’s Expertise** by **15%.**

## _Radiant Rarity_

- _Braided Line - “A stronger, higher quality bow string.“_
  - Increases the damage of **Hunter’s Expertise** by **15** and increases the speed of **Arrows** by **40%.**
- _Honing Stone - “The razor-sharp blades inflict deep wounds aimed at bleeding the victim as fast as possible.“_
  - Survivors who step into beatraps are afflicted with **Bleeding** for **6 Seconds** and **Fatal Wounds.** Survivors who escape a beartrap themselves will be instantly set down to **40% Max HP**, otherwise the base damage **+25% Extra Damage.** Will only deal it’s normal damage amount if rescued by another Survivor.
- _Invisible Ink - “Invisible ink that sticks to anything it comes into contact with, allowing for covert tracking.“_
  - Resetting a disarmed beartrap will reveal the **Aura** of the Survivor who disarmed it for **5 Seconds.**
- _Tension Spring - “A high tension spring that snaps back into place after being stretched.“_
  - When a Survivor escapes a beartrap, the trap will reset itself **2 Seconds** later.
- _Barbed Arrowhead - “A barbed arrowhead, causing much more deep injuries.“_
  - **Hunter’s Expertise** now inflicts **Deep Wounds.**
- _Trapper Sack - “A large sack, allowing for the carrying of much more equipment.“_
  - Start with **3 Extra Beartraps** and **+2 Tripwire Limit.**
- _Back Quiver - “A large quiver worn on the back, allowing for much more arrow carrying capacity.“_
  - Lowers the cooldown of **Hunter’s Expertise** by **25%.**
- _Alchemy Belt - “A belt with many hooks, allowing the wearer to carry as many bottles as they need.“_
  - Lowers the cooldown of **Lace** by **20%.**
- _Tripod Bag - “A bag designed for holding recording equipment.“_
  - Increases the placement limit of **Game Cameras** by **2.**
- _Punji Coating - “A toxic substance used to coat traps that reacts with blood in volatile ways and induces rapid infection and organ failure.”_
  - Beartraps and Tripwires now inflict **Poison I** for **8 Seconds.** Four seconds after applying **Poison,** apply **Nausea** for **4 Seconds.**

## _Ethereal Rarity_

- _Bloody Coils - “Coils coated in a grim, sinister fluid that causes the handling and disarming of traps a dangerous task.”_
  - When a **Healthy Survivor** disarms a beartrap, instantly put them into the **Injured State.**
- _Bloody Wires - “Wires coated in a grim, sinister fluid that causes the handling and disarming of traps a dangerous task.”_
  - When a **Healthy Survivor** disarms a tripwire, instantly afflict them with **Bleed II** for **15 Seconds.**
- _HE Flare - “A flare filled with an unstable concoction and powder, causing it’s activation to become a violent explosion.”_
  - Lowers the **Tripwire Limit** by **4.** When a Survivor triggers a tripwire now, the flare attached will explode after **1.5 Seconds,** dealing **40 Damage** to all Survivors within **20 Studs** and inflicting **Crippled** for **6 Seconds.**
- _Ethereal Stone - “A mysterious stone, emitting a sickening and ominous fog; giving what seems to be life to objects designed for harm.”_
  - Tripwires are no longer destroyed upon being triggered. Every **15 Seconds**, reset and fix a random disarmed beartrap and tripwire.
- _Iridescent Arrowhead - “An arrowhead full of an iridescent, sickly stone; giving off a bright, sinister gleam.”_
  - Increases the cooldown of **Hunter’s Expertise** by **100%.** Survivors hit by the arrows are now instantly put into the **Dying State.**
- _Iridescent Camera Lens - “An odd, watching lens; seemingly having a consciousness of it’s own as the faint, opaque eyes within lens looks around.”_
  - **Game Camera** **Limit** is reduced by **2 Placements.** Survivors revealed by the cameras now have their **Aura Revealed** for **30 Seconds.**
cards.name: Hunter

cards.desc:
# **Nurse’s Refractions**

## _Ashen Rarity_

- _Torn Gown - “Once worn by a patient known for their outbreaks.“_
  - Grants +100% bonus Fog Essence for _Sporadic Attack_ Score Events.
  - Removes final token fatigue avoidance and Standard Attack lockout. _(Meaning; Must attack when available or suffer full fatigue. Spending final token will no longer lock you out of using your Standard Attack.)_
  - Removes Oblivious and Exhausted from _“Sporadicity.”_
  - Sets _“Sporadicity”_ tokens to 2.
- _Tattered Scrubs - “An old and tattered pair of Scrubs from the older days within the Institute.“_
  - Increases the minimum distance of each blink in _“Sporadicity”_ by 1 Stud.
- _Fractured Cryovial - “A fractured remnant of what used to be a vial used for testing blood.“_
  - Increase the amount of Blood Count gained from Survivor Healing by 25.
- _Empty Disinfectant - “An empty bottle lost in the back of the supply closet.“_
  - Increases _“A Nurse’s Calling“_ secondary passive healing by 1h/s.
- _Unrecognizable Crochet - “A bundle of yarn that has long since been ruined, unrecognizable as to what it used to be, knitted by an older patient for Kathy as one of many gifts.“_
  - Reduce the Base Fatigue Duration incurred from missed Sporadic Attacks by 0.5 seconds.

## _Dissonant Rarity_

- _Bloody Towelette - “A towelette soaked in dried blood, found in the dead, cold hands of one of the patients. Appeared to have been trying to wipe their own blood up.“_
  - Increases the Bleeding duration and the ability cooldown of _“Sporadicity“_ by 2 Seconds.
- _Dried Cryovial - “A vial with remnants of blood, dried and stuck to it’s interior with an acrid stench.“_
  - Increases max Blood Count by 500.
  - Increase the amount of Blood Count gained from Survivor Healing by 50.
- _Broken Camera - “An old camera that was linked to the old security system of the Institute.“_
  - Increases _“Stridor“_ volume boost by 50%.
- _Refraction4 - “Lore“_
  - Reduce _“Sporadicity”_ cooldown by 5 seconds.
- _Dusty Crochet Turtle - “A crochet turtle made by the sweet old woman of Room 104.“_
  - Reduce the additional Fatigue from Chain Fades by 50%.

## _Luminous Rarity_

- _Solemn Words - “The last words spoken by a patient known as the Crybaby.“_
  - Adds an extra token to _“Sporadicity.“_
- _Empty Cryovial - “A vial in good condition.“_
  - Increases max Blood Count by 750.
  - Increase the amount of Blood Count gained from Survivor Healing by 75
- _Disabled Camera - “A spare camera kept in the back storage of the Institute, never having been installed.“_
  - Increases _“Stridor“_ volume boost by 100%.
- _Jesse’s Blood - “A vial containing the blood of a young, terribly ill patient; found only after their late passing.“_
  - _“Intravasation“_ now inflicts Broken for 45 seconds.
- _Edith’s Blood - “A vial containing the blood of a patient who had a condition known as Thalassemias.“_
  - Increases the duration of Anaphylaxis by 4 seconds.

## _Radiant Rarity_

- _His Breath - “The last breath Felix Woods took, transformed and sealed within a flask.“_
  - Reduce **Terror Radius** to **65 Studs** when _“Sporadicity“_ is unavailable.
- _Phlebotomy Sharps Container - “A large, empty container used to store blood.“_
  - Increases max Blood Count by 1,500.
  - Increase the amount of Blood Count gained from Survivor Healing by 150.
- _Luminol - “A chemical used in crime scenes to detect blood.“_
  - Reveal the Aura of any Survivor afflicted with any of the following Status Effects for 10 seconds from the time of affliction; _Bleeding, Haemorrhage, Deep Wounds, Fatal Wounds, Broken_
- _Bloodied Coat - “The coat of Felix Woods found at the scene of his death.“_
  - When performing a Sporadic Attack during the last available token of _“Sporadicity,”_ increase the duration of your lunge by 0.3 seconds.

## _Ethereal Rarity_

- _Felix’s Memento - “The larynx of the late Felix Woods, now dried and cleaned of his vile and tainted blood._
  _It seems to be exhaling a black fog.“_
  - Reduces your Terror Radius to 90 Studs.
  - Reduces your Standard Attack Damage to 25.
  - Sets the token amount of _“Sporadicity”_ to 3.
  - Sets the recharge cooldown of _“Sporadicity“_ to 3 seconds.
  - Sets the range of _“Sporadicity“_ to 10 Studs.
  - Sets the Base Fatigue of _“Sporadicity”_ by 1 second.
  - Sets the Chain Fade Fatigue to 0.3 seconds.
  - Sets the Sporadic Attack Fatigue to 0.7 seconds.
  - _“Sporadicity”_ now also inflicts Blindness for 10 seconds.
  - Successful Sporadic Attacks inflict Darkness for 10 seconds.
- _Lifeblood - “A collection of blood, untainted and pure of any contaminants, arcane in origin. It does not stain, it does not age, and it cannot be compromised by any means.“_
  - Increases passive lifesteal to 100% but sets passive regeneration to 0h/s.
  - Increases the duration of all negative status effects inflicted from abilities by 5 seconds.
  - _“Sporadicity“_ no longer fatigues you, but removes the ability to perform a Sporadic Attack.
  - _“Sporadicity“_ now cleanses all negative status effects effecting you on use.
  - Reduce the recharge cooldown of _“Sporadicity”_ by 1 second.
  - Increases the token amount of _“Sporadicity”_ by 2.

cards.desc:
# **Ash Ferris Wilson**

## _"My choices may not always be wise, and my actions may not always result in a smooth victory; but I do the best I can within my abilities."_

### **Profile**

`Age:` `24`
`Birthdate:` `August 25th`
`Origin:` `Ireland`
`Gender:` `Cis Female`

A rugged Irish woman wearing a beat-up brown leather coat, black undershirt, denim jeans, and black work boots. She also notably has bloodied bandages around her right shoulder.

Her hair is black and in the style of a bixie. She has notable scarring on her left cheek and deep hazel eyes, glaring with a sense of purpose and a steadfast visage.

_"Originating from a slum ridden with disease and death, Ash stood as a beacon of hope; a halcyon in the damp, dark reality that was life in the Terminal. She stood up for those unable to fight for themselves, fighting back against the system that put all of them into this position; advocating for a revolution, doing her best with what she had. Doing everything to instill a sense of purpose within everyone in the Terminal who had already lost all hope."_

## **Overview**

Ash is a great team player kind of character, benefiting _heavily_ from working with your team to acquire tokens for her passive, and benefiting further by using your abilities selflessly rather than for yourself. She can create amazing openings for your teammates to get to safety, while being able to last in a fight herself while doing so; gathering Overhealth and Resistance as she progresses.

`Idle Animation:` Looking around slowly, breathing in and out.

`Walking Animation:` Default.

`Sprinting Animation:` Default.

### **Pros & Cons**

\+Great Teamwork Potential
\+Tanky with Passive
\+Chase Time Potential
\+Difficult to Ignore
\+Higher Health
\+Big Payoff with Halcyon
\+Insane Resource Potential
\+Good Debuffing

±Ability Requires Timing

\-Low Stamina Regen
\-Passive Takes Time To Build
\-Tokens Can be Lost Quickly
\-Very Punishable

### **Stats**

- `Archetype: Altruistic`
- `Sub-Archetype: Confrontational`
- `Difficulty: 1/5 - Beginner`
- `Health: 220`
- `Stamina: 8`
- `Stamina Regen: 0.65`

## **Obtainment**

- _Unlocked by Default_

## **Loadout**

### _Mentioned_ [_Status Effects_](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌"):

- _Endurance_ - Negates all damage taken when hit and instead afflicts the Survivor with _Fatal Wounds_ and removes this status. If the attack does not deal over **20 Damage**, only Negate the damage and do not proc _Fatal Wounds_. _On-Hit Haste is still applied when hit by any ability that procs it._
- _Fatal Wounds_ - Starts a **15 Second** timer that will pause while running, sprinting, or performing a **Mend Action** on self. Once the **15 Second** timer is up, the **Survivor** afflicted will be downed. **Fatal Wounds** can be cleansed by performing a **Mend Action** on yourself or have another **Survivor** mend you. _Mend Actions on self are at 33% Healing Speed and will benefit from Healing Speed Proficiency._
- _Resistance_ - Flat percentage damage resistance. Listed as a % when present.
- _Overhealth_ - Extra health applied on top of current HP. No special effects.

### **Perks**

_**Protector [1 Point]**_ | _“You acquire a sense of danger and a need to protect.“_
Power awakens in your Aura-reading ability.

- Whenever a Survivor is put into the **Injured State**, their **Aura** is revealed to you for **8/10/12 Seconds.** If you are within **30 Studs** of the Survivor when this effect activates, grant them **20 Overhealth** that decays over **6 Seconds.**

**Protection Hits** reward **50%/75%/100%** more **Fog Essence.**

_**Fáth le bheith ann [3 Points]**_ | _“Fáth bheatha Luaith, ag spreagadh daoine eile chun seasamh suas agus troid.“_
Whenever another Survivor is **Injured**, gain the following effects for **6 Seconds:**

- **+25%/37.5%/50% Haste**

Afterwards, become **Exhausted** for **30 Seconds.**

_**Never Back Down [2 Points]**_ | _“You feel compelled to aid others, in any way that you can, no matter what.“_
When a Survivor is downed, gain the following effects for **30/45/60 Seconds:**

- **+100% Altruistic Healing Speed**

### **Mastery Perk**

_**Bulwark**_ | _“And I’ll be damned if I’m the last one here!“_
This Perk will active while in the **Injured State:**

- Using the **Active Perk** ability key will cause you to dash forward a short distance and trigger the [**Endurance**](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌") **Status Effect** for **0.6 Seconds.**
- Bulwark causes the **Exhausted Status Effect** for **40 Seconds.**

Afterwards, Bulwark goes on cooldown for **90 Seconds.**
_Bulwark cannot be used while Exhausted._

### `Passive:` `Firm As A Mountain`

Whenever Ash takes **Protection Hits** or completes **Altruistic Heals**, this passive gains **Tokens**, up to a maximum of **10.**

- **Protection Hits: 3 Tokens**
- **Altruistic Heals: 1 Token**

For every **Token** earned, Ash gains **1 Overhealth.**
For every **5 Tokens** earned, Ash gains a permanent stack of **Endurance.**
For every **Token** above **0**, Ash gains **3% Resistance.**
At **7+ Tokens**, Ash becomes immune to **Fatal Wounds.**
At **10+ Tokens,** all other Survivors within **100 Studs** are given **half** of your **Resistance Status.**

Ash loses **2 Tokens** when hit by a **Standard Attack.**
Ash loses **3 Tokens** if she takes an **Endurance Hit** and cleanses **Fatal Wounds** using the 7+ Token immunity.

### `AB1/Q:` `Halcyon`

On use, Ash shields herself with her arm for **0.75 Seconds.** If hit at close-range during this, she will immediately **Parry**; negating all damage, knocking the attacker back **15 Studs** _(+100% Distance if Entity),_ and applying all following effects;

- Deal **50 Damage** + **200% Damage Negated** back to the Attacker
- Cleanse **All Ailments** on self
- **+50% Haste** for **2 Seconds**
- **Stun** attacker for **1 Second** _(+100% Stun Time if Entity)_.

If this ability is used for a **Protection Hit**, extend all positive effects that Ash gains listed above to all nearby allies within **50 Studs** and double the stun time.

If the ability fails to activate, Ash will incur a **20% Hinder** for **1.75 Seconds.**

_Will not cause “Firm As A Mountain” to lose stacks. Can count as a “Protection Hit.”_

- _60s Cooldown_

### `AB2/E:` `Rules of the Terminal`

Ash bends down, placing her hands on the ground before dashing forward a short distance, sweeping with her feet. On hit, trip the Killer, inflicting **Helpless** for **3 Seconds** and **25% Weakness** for **10 Seconds.**

Ash cannot be damaged during the dash, but can be hit with **Grabs.**

- _40s Cooldown_

### `AB3/F:` `Resourceful`

On use, consume **2-4 Scrap/3-5 Rusted Scrap** to craft a random amount of items.

The amount of items that are crafted range from **3-5** depending on how much scrap was used.

The item pool of items that are crafted are listed below:

- _Random Rarity Medkit_
- _Random Rarity Toolbox_
- _2x~4x Lock-picks_
- _1x~2x Flash-bangs_
- _1x~2x Trip-mines_
- _1x~2x Grenades_
- _1x~2x Molotov's_
- _12x~20x Pistol Ammo_
- _6x~12x Revolver Ammo_
- _5x~8x Shotgun Ammo_
- _3x~6x Rifle Ammo_

The process for this ability takes **5 Seconds** to finish, and is relatively loud. Ash is slowed down by **65%** during the animation, and cannot cancel it. If Ash is hit by an attack during the animation, it will be instantly cancelled.

- _90s Cooldown_

## **Mastery Achievements**

- _Achievement 1 - Take 150 Protection Hits_
- _Achievement 2 - Parry the Killer 100 Times using Halcyon_
- _Achievement 3 - Trip the Killer 250 Times using Rules of the Terminal_
- _Achievement 4 - Craft 1,000 Items using Resourceful_
- _Achievement 5 - Escape 100 Times_

## **Skins**

- _**[Mastery] Capable** - Time-skip Ash, bloodied and donning a prosthetic arm. Much more scarred._
cards.name: Ash Ferris Wilson

cards.desc:
# **Jordyn Stave**

## _"Where the hell could he have gone..? I’ve been walking through here for hours.. it’s getting cold."_

### **Profile**

`Age:` `19`
`Birthdate:` `13th February`
`Origin:` `American`
`Gender:` `Cis Male`

A short, white-haired boy wearing a dark purple hoodie with a star symbol on the back of it and black skinny jeans. He also wears two black flat platform with buckles lining it, as well as side-zip closures.
He is wearing two earrings, one on each ear; the left ear being of a star and the right being a cross. The first earring is made of silver, while the second is black titanium.
There is a white-inked tattoo displaying _“M.G.,”_ on his wrist, the initials of his partner.
He also has a gold necklace with a half-heart around his neck.

On his right hand, he wears a black glove with a pulsing purple star on it’s palm; radiating with an arcane energy.

_"Lore Excerpt"_

## **Overview**

Jordyn is a very flexible Survivor with a lot of Survivability and Stealth Potential, although suffers from the downside of being forced to charge his ability over time, which will soak up a lot of his use cases in-game, but once he has his ability charged and begins to pick up the pace in terms of equipment, Jordyn can prove to be a powerful asset in terms of damage and utility despite his difficulty.

`Idle Animation:` Stands with his gloved hand against his chest.

`Walking Animation:` Default.

`Sprinting Animation:` Default.

### **Pros & Cons**

\+Great Passive for Rune, Glyph, and Ritual Perks
\+Flexible Ability, Many Use Cases

\-Arcana Must Be Charged
\-Long Cooldowns/Ability Downtime
\-Little Team-play Potential Outside of Perks & Imbue Soul Damage

### **Stats**

- `Archetype: Preservation`
- `Sub-Archetype: Stealth`
- `Difficulty: 4/5 - Expert`
- `Health: 175`
- `Stamina: 8`
- `Stamina Regen: 0.7`

## **Obtainment**

- _Complete the_ [_Tutorial_](https://trello.com/c/Kowp6KN2/400-into-the-unknown-free-tutorial "‌")

## **Loadout**

### _Mentioned_ [_Status Effects_](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌"):

Status Effects that appear within this Character’s Kit.

- _Grace_ - Negates all damage taken when hit, then remove a stack of this Status. Can be quantified in a duration timer or in stacks, stacks lasting until the on-hit effect is proc’d. _On-Hit Haste is still applied when hit by any ability that procs it._
- _Silenced_ - Silences footsteps, Loud Noise Notifications, and all ambient noises _(Ambient Killer Noises, Survivor Breathing, & Grunts of Pain)._
- _Haste_ - Movement Speed increase. Listed as a % when present.
- _Peril_ - Survivors are instantly put into the **Dying State** upon being hit by a **Standard Attack** regardless of **Health**.
- _Power_ - Damage increase. Listed as a % when present.
- _Concealed_ - Conceals all actions and blocks all Aura-reading. Also applies the following effects to the effected role:
  - _(Killer)_ Hides Terror Radius/Heartbeat.
  - _(Killer)_ Hides Red Stain.
  - _(Survivor)_ Hides Shadow Trails.

### **Perks**

_**Rune: Locket [4 Points]**_ | _“A keepsake of a dearly beloved, it radiates warmth.“_
Contains **1 Charge.**
Upon taking **Fatal Damage**, immediately gain [**Grace**](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌") for **1/2/3 Seconds** and cleanse all **Status Effects**, positive and negative, and subtract a Charge from the Rune.

_**Glyph: Silence [3 Points]**_ | _“Sometimes silence is the best medicine. Just stick around.“_
Interact with a **Dormant Inscription** or **Sigil** to **bless** it and create a **Glyph.**
_Glyph: Silence_ has a **60 Stud Radius.**
All **Survivors** benefit from the following effects while inside the **Glyph’s Radius**:

- _Silenced Status_
- _Suppresses_ [_Shadows_](https://trello.com/c/o2q6CYpH/155-shadows-of-the-fog "‌")
  - Both effects will persist for **2/4/6 Seconds** after leaving the Glyph’s radius.

_**Ritual: Dark Omen [4 Points]**_ | _“An inexplicable feeling of impending doom is a warning, not paranoia.“_
Interact with a **Ritual Circle** to begin performing a _Ritual_, which takes **120 Seconds.**
During a Ritual, your **Aura** is revealed to all Survivors, who can join the Ritual, accelerating it by **+100%** if they have a Ritual Perk or **+50%** if they do not.
Once the Ritual is complete, the following effects are applied:

- When the **Killer** is within **100 Studs** of a Survivor and looking at them with a clear _Line-of-Sight_, that Survivor will hear an auditory warning and gain a **+8% Action Speed Bonus** for **Vaulting** and **Wedging**, alongside **+2% Haste** for **4 Seconds**, then the effect will go on **Cooldown** for **20/18/16 Seconds.** _(Cooldowns are independent for each Survivor.)_
- You are **permanently** afflicted with [**Peril**](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌") and **+4%** [**Haste**](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌").

### **Mastery Perk**

_**Retribution**_ | _“Retribution comes.“_
The following effects apply while you are within **17 Studs** of a **Killer** carrying a **Survivor**:

- _Grants you **+5%**_ [_**Haste**_](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌")
- _Increases the carried Survivor’s Wiggling Speed by **+10%**_
- _Survivors who successfully wiggle out while you are within the radius are granted **+25%**_ [_**Power**_](https://trello.com/c/gt3JhP4h/18-status-effects-status-hud "‌") _for **15 Seconds**_
